incorrect: Women were completely excluded from all social and economic activities
Women were required to wear veils and follow Middle Eastern customs
Women had no role whatsoever in trade or economic activities

questions.text: Mansa Musa's famous hajj to Mecca was significant because:
correct: His lavish displays of gold in Cairo demonstrated Mali's immense wealth and put the West African kingdom on international maps
incorrect: He was the first African ruler in history to make the pilgrimage to Mecca
He established permanent diplomatic relations with all Middle Eastern kingdoms
He converted the entire population of Mali to Islam during his absence from the kingdom

questions.text: Mali's wealth was derived from: (Select all that apply)
correct: Control of trans-Saharan gold trade routes
Agricultural production in fertile regions along the Niger River
Taxation of salt and copper trade passing through their territory
Strategic position controlling trade between North and sub-Saharan Africa
incorrect: Large-scale manufacturing of textiles for international export
Maritime trade and naval operations in the Atlantic Ocean

questions.text: The kingdom of Axum's significance in Ethiopian history included:
correct: Early adoption of Christianity and establishment of trade connections with Rome, Arabia, and India
incorrect: Complete isolation from all foreign influences and international trade
Exclusive focus on Islamic religious practices and Middle Eastern connections
Systematic rejection of all forms of international trade and commerce

questions.text: The 11 rock churches built in 12th century Ethiopia demonstrated:
correct: Monumental architecture requiring enormous resources, skilled craftsmanship, and organized labor
incorrect: Simple wooden structures designed for temporary seasonal use
Direct imitation of European cathedral architectural designs
Purely Islamic architectural influences and building techniques

questions.text: Ethiopian Christianity from the 12th-16th centuries was characterized by:
correct: Independent development separated from both Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches
incorrect: Direct control and administration by the Pope in Rome
Complete adoption of Byzantine Orthodox practices and theology
Systematic rejection of all Christian beliefs and practices

questions.text: The Kingdom of Ethiopia was unique in medieval Africa because of which characteristic?
correct: Christian kingdom that successfully resisted Islamic expansion
incorrect: Isolated kingdom with no external trade contacts
Nomadic empire following seasonal migration patterns

questions.text: Ethiopian Christianity differed from European Christianity in which ways?
correct: Oriental Orthodox traditions rather than Roman Catholic
Integration with local African customs and practices
Use of Ge'ez language for religious texts
incorrect: Rejection of Jesus Christ as divine figure
Worship of multiple gods alongside Christianity

questions.text: The rock-hewn churches of Lalibela in Ethiopia were constructed during which period and for what purpose?
correct: 12th-13th centuries as a 'New Jerusalem' pilgrimage site
incorrect: Ancient times as pagan temples later converted
Modern period using European architectural techniques
As defensive fortifications against Muslim armies

questions.text: Ethiopia's geographic location provided which advantages for trade and defense?
correct: Highland terrain difficult for enemies to invade
Access to Red Sea trade routes
Control of sources of the Blue Nile
incorrect: Atlantic coastal ports for European trade
Desert barriers protecting from all invasion

questions.text: The legend of Prester John in medieval European thought was connected to which African kingdom?
correct: Ethiopia - Europeans believed it was ruled by a legendary Christian king
incorrect: Mali - thought to be a Christian empire in West Africa
Great Zimbabwe - mistaken for a European-founded colony
Swahili city-states - assumed to be Christian trading posts

African_Social_Structures_and_Cultural_P.name: African Social Structures and Cultural Patterns

questions.text: Kin-based societies in sub-Saharan Africa were organized around: (Select all that apply)
correct: Extended families and clans as the foundation of social and economic organization
Communal land ownership rather than individual private property
Age grades that created social bonds crossing family lines
Gender-based division of labor with specialized roles
incorrect: Individual ownership of land and all forms of property
Complete social equality without any hierarchical structures

questions.text: The Zanj Rebellion (869-883 CE) was significant because it:
correct: Demonstrated enslaved people's determination to escape brutal conditions and was one of history's most successful slave revolts
incorrect: Successfully ended all forms of slavery throughout the Islamic world
Led to immediate abolition of the trans-Saharan slave trade
Had no lasting impact on slavery practices or policies

questions.text: Griots and griottes in African societies served as:
correct: Oral historians and storytellers who preserved community history and transmitted cultural knowledge across generations
incorrect: Military leaders and warriors responsible for defense
Religious priests and spiritual leaders exclusively

TransSaharan_Trade_and_West_African_King.name: Trans-Saharan Trade and West African Kingdoms

questions.text: The Kingdom of Ghana (300-1200 CE) prospered primarily through control of which trade commodity?
correct: Gold from mines in the south traded for salt from the north
incorrect: Ivory from elephants hunted in the savanna
Slaves captured in wars with neighboring kingdoms
Copper from mines in the desert regions

questions.text: The Mali Empire (1235-1600 CE) succeeded Ghana and became even more powerful due to which advantages?
correct: Strategic location on trans-Saharan trade routes
Adoption of Islam facilitating trade with North Africa
incorrect: Alliance with Christian kingdoms in Ethiopia

questions.text: Mansa Musa's famous pilgrimage to Mecca (1324-1325) demonstrated which aspects of Mali's wealth and power?
correct: Enormous quantities of gold distributed in Cairo and Mecca
Mali's integration into the broader Islamic world
Administrative capability to organize massive expedition
incorrect: Conversion of all subjects to Islam

questions.text: Trans-Saharan trade was made possible by which technological and organizational innovations?
correct: Camel caravans adapted to desert travel
Knowledge of oasis locations and water sources
Islamic commercial law facilitating long-distance trade

questions.text: The Songhai Empire eventually replaced Mali as the dominant West African power and controlled which strategic location?
correct: Timbuktu and Gao along the Niger River bend
incorrect: Coastal ports along the Atlantic Ocean
Mountain passes through the Atlas Mountains
Desert oases in the central Sahara

Great_Zimbabwe_and_Southern_African_Stat.name: Great Zimbabwe and Southern African States

questions.text: Great Zimbabwe (1100-1450 CE) was significant as which type of African state?
correct: Major trading state controlling gold trade routes to the coast
incorrect: Religious center for pilgrimage and worship

questions.text: The massive stone structures at Great Zimbabwe served which functions?
correct: Symbol of state power and authority

questions.text: Great Zimbabwe's wealth derived primarily from which economic activities?
correct: Gold mining and trade with coastal ports
Control of trade routes to Indian Ocean
incorrect: Manufacturing of iron tools and weapons

questions.text: The architectural achievement of Great Zimbabwe demonstrated which capabilities?
correct: Engineering skills for large-scale building projects
incorrect: Use of metal tools imported from Asia

questions.text: The decline of Great Zimbabwe around 1450 CE may have resulted from which factors?
correct: Shift in trade routes to other coastal ports

questions.text: The wealth of the Mali Empire, exemplified by Mansa Musa's famous Hajj, was primarily derived from...
correct: control of trans-Saharan trade in gold and salt
incorrect: extensive maritime trade networks in the Indian Ocean
vast agricultural surplus from Champa rice cultivation
large-scale production of silk and porcelain for export

questions.text: Mansa Musa's hajj and the city of Timbuktu helped to demonstrate:
correct: The wealth of Mali and Timbuktu's role as a center of Islamic learning and trade
incorrect: That Mali had no involvement in long-distance trade
That Timbuktu was isolated from the Islamic world
That Mansa Musa was an opponent of Islamic scholarship

questions.text: Great Zimbabwe's economy was built primarily on:
correct: Control of interior trade routes and the regional gold trade linked to the Indian Ocean world
incorrect: An economy based only on whale oil

questions.text: Swahili city-states developed because of:
correct: Indian Ocean trade, diasporic merchant communities, and access to luxury exports (ivory, gold, slaves)
incorrect: Complete dependence on Trans-Saharan caravans only
Absence of any foreign merchant communities

questions.text: European feudalism was characterized by:
correct: Decentralized political authority based on personal relationships, land tenure, and military service obligations
incorrect: Centralized monarchies with strong national governments and professional bureaucracies
Democratic institutions with elected representatives and popular participation
Complete political anarchy without any organized authority or leadership

questions.text: The manorial system organized agricultural production through:
correct: Self-sufficient estates where serfs worked the lord's land in exchange for protection and small plots for subsistence
incorrect: Large-scale commercial farms producing crops for distant urban markets
Completely independent peasant farmers owning their own land outright
Nomadic herding without any permanent agricultural settlements

questions.text: Serfdom differed from slavery in that serfs:
correct: Were bound to the land but had some legal rights and could not be sold separately from the estate
incorrect: Were completely free to leave their lords and move anywhere at any time
Were identical to slaves in all legal and social respects
Had no obligations whatsoever to their lords or the manor

questions.text: Feudalism in medieval Europe was characterized by which political and social arrangements?
correct: Hierarchical system of personal loyalty and land tenure between lords and vassals

questions.text: The manor system provided the economic foundation for feudalism through which arrangement?
correct: Self-sufficient agricultural estates worked by serfs for noble lords
incorrect: Urban workshops producing goods for international trade
Nomadic herding following seasonal migration patterns
Commercial agriculture producing crops for market sale

questions.text: Serfdom differed from slavery in which important ways?
correct: Serfs were bound to the land rather than owned as property
Serfs had some legal rights and could not be sold separately from land
Serfs owed labor obligations but kept some of their agricultural production
incorrect: Serfs could freely move between different manors
Serfs received wages for their agricultural labor

questions.text: Political decentralization in medieval Europe resulted from which factors?
correct: Collapse of centralized Roman imperial authority
Lack of money economy to support standing armies
Geographic barriers and poor transportation systems

questions.text: The feudal contract between lord and vassal involved which mutual obligations?
correct: Lord provided land (fief) and protection to vassal
Vassal provided military service and loyalty to lord
Both parties swore oaths of mutual obligation
incorrect: Lord paid wages to vassal for military service
Vassal had right to elect or remove the lord

Role_of_the_Roman_Catholic_Church_ndds.name: Role of the Roman Catholic Church

questions.text: The Roman Catholic Church's influence on medieval European society included: (Select all that apply)
correct: Providing education and literacy through monastery schools and cathedral schools
Serving as the primary source of written records, books, and preserved knowledge
Offering social services including care for the poor, sick, and travelers
Legitimizing political authority through religious ceremonies and divine sanction
incorrect: Promoting complete separation of church and state in all political matters
Encouraging unrestricted scientific inquiry without any religious limitations

questions.text: The Crusades represented:
correct: Military expeditions sanctioned by the Church to reclaim the Holy Land from Muslim control
incorrect: Peaceful diplomatic missions and cultural exchanges with the Middle East
Internal European wars between competing Christian kingdoms
Exploration voyages to discover new continents and trade routes

questions.text: The Great Schism of 1054 resulted in:
correct: Permanent division between Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox Christianity
incorrect: Reunification of all Christian churches under papal authority
Complete abandonment of Christianity in Eastern Europe
Mass conversion of the Byzantine Empire to Islam

questions.text: Key differences between Roman Catholicism and Eastern Orthodoxy included: (Select all that apply)
correct: Papal authority versus conciliar church governance
Use of Latin versus Greek in religious services
Different theological positions on the nature of the Trinity
Clerical celibacy requirements versus married priests
incorrect: Belief in completely different gods and deities
Complete rejection of the Bible and Christian scriptures

Role_of_Christianity_in_Medieval_Europe_.name: Role of Christianity in Medieval Europe

questions.text: The Roman Catholic Church was the most powerful institution in medieval Europe because it controlled which aspects of society?
correct: Large amounts of land and economic resources

questions.text: The Great Schism of 1054 CE divided Christianity into which two major branches?
correct: Roman Catholicism in Western Europe and Eastern Orthodoxy in Byzantine Empire
incorrect: Monastic orders and secular church hierarchy

questions.text: Medieval monasteries served which important functions in European society?
correct: Preservation of classical learning and texts
Provision of education and literacy training

questions.text: The Crusades (1095-1291) were launched by the Catholic Church for which stated purposes?
correct: Recapture of Jerusalem and Holy Land from Muslim control
Defense of Byzantine Empire against Islamic expansion
incorrect: Conversion of all Muslims to Christianity
Establishment of European colonies in Asia

questions.text: Christianity shaped medieval European society by promoting which values and practices?
correct: Hierarchical social order reflecting divine plan
Charity and care for the poor and sick
Moral codes governing personal and social behavior

questions.text: The Byzantine Empire represented which continuation of classical civilization?
correct: Eastern Roman Empire preserving Greco-Roman traditions with Christianity
incorrect: Pure Greek culture without Roman influences
Germanic tribal confederation in Eastern Europe

questions.text: Constantinople's strategic location provided the Byzantine Empire with which advantages?
correct: Control of trade routes between Europe and Asia
Access to both Mediterranean and Black Sea regions
incorrect: Rich agricultural lands for food production
Mountain barriers protecting from all invasion

questions.text: Eastern Orthodox Christianity differed from Roman Catholicism in which theological and organizational aspects?
correct: Rejection of papal supremacy and authority
Different views on the Holy Spirit's procession
Married clergy allowed in local parishes
incorrect: Rejection of Jesus Christ as divine figure

questions.text: Byzantine cultural achievements included which developments?
correct: Preservation of classical Greek and Roman texts
Creation of illuminated manuscripts and religious art
incorrect: Invention of printing press and movable type

questions.text: The eventual fall of the Byzantine Empire to the Ottoman Turks in 1453 resulted from which factors?
correct: Gradual loss of territory to Islamic expansion
Economic decline and reduced military capacity
Internal political instability and civil wars

questions.text: Medieval European agriculture was based primarily on which type of production system?
correct: Subsistence farming with limited surplus for trade
incorrect: Commercial agriculture producing crops for export

questions.text: The three-field system represented which agricultural innovation in medieval Europe?
correct: Crop rotation leaving one field fallow to restore soil fertility
incorrect: Irrigation systems bringing water to dry regions

questions.text: Social organization in medieval Europe was primarily determined by which factor?
correct: Land ownership and agricultural production relationships

questions.text: The medieval European social hierarchy consisted of which major groups?
correct: Those who fight (nobility), those who pray (clergy), those who work (peasants)
incorrect: Urban dwellers, rural farmers, and nomadic herders
Free citizens, slaves, and foreign immigrants

questions.text: Limited technological innovation in medieval European agriculture resulted from which factors?
correct: Lack of capital investment in new techniques
Traditional methods passed down through generations
Limited contact with more advanced agricultural regions
incorrect: Religious prohibition against new farming methods

questions.text: The political system of Europe during most of this period (c. 1200-1450) is best described as...
correct: decentralized feudalism, where land was exchanged for loyalty and military service
incorrect: a unified empire under the supreme authority of the Pope
centralized bureaucratic nation-states with absolute monarchs
a democratic federation of independent city-states

questions.text: Which innovation most directly improved long-distance maritime navigation?
correct: Magnetic compass (plus astrolabe and improved ship designs)

questions.text: The Pax Mongolica is best described as:
correct: A period of safer and more connected trade routes under Mongol rule, facilitating cross-regional exchange
incorrect: A code of law banning trade
A permanent political union of Europe and Asia

questions.text: Which environmental/biological diffusion was a negative consequence of expanding trade networks?
correct: Spread of the bubonic plague along trade routes
incorrect: Introducing horses to South America before 1200

questions.text: Which of the following were important goods moved along the Indian Ocean trade network? (Select all that apply)
incorrect: Large numbers of European manufactured firearms

state_formation_patterns.name: Patterns of State Formation and Governance

questions.text: Centralized versus decentralized political systems can be compared as follows:
correct: Song China and Inca Empire were centralized with extensive bureaucracies; Aztec Empire and European feudalism were decentralized with tributary or feudal relationships
incorrect: All Asian states were decentralized while all American states were centralized
All Islamic states were centralized while all Christian states were decentralized
All states during this period adopted identical centralized political structures

questions.text: Labor systems across different regions included: (Select all that apply)
correct: Corvée labor in China for infrastructure projects like the Grand Canal
Mita system in the Inca Empire for state service and public works
Serfdom in Europe binding peasants to manorial estates
Tribute labor in the Aztec Empire from conquered peoples
incorrect: Identical labor systems across all civilizations worldwide
Complete absence of organized labor in all societies

questions.text: Trade-based wealth was the foundation of power for: (Select all that apply)
correct: Srivijaya Empire controlling Southeast Asian maritime routes
Great Zimbabwe taxing gold transport to coastal trading cities
Mali Empire controlling trans-Saharan trade networks
Swahili city-states serving as Indian Ocean commercial ports
incorrect: Inca Empire focusing primarily on maritime commerce
European feudal lords controlling international trade networks

questions.text: Syncretism in religious practices was demonstrated by: (Select all that apply)
correct: Ethiopian Christianity incorporating traditional ancestor veneration and spirit beliefs
Islam in Mali blending with traditional African practices and matrilineal inheritance
Buddhism in Southeast Asia coexisting with Hindu elements at Angkor Wat
Neo-Confucianism in China incorporating Buddhist and Daoist metaphysical ideas
incorrect: Complete replacement of all local traditions by foreign religions
Universal rejection of all religious practices in favor of secular governance

questions.text: Mystical religious movements like Sufism and the Bhakti Movement shared:
correct: Emphasis on personal spiritual experience accessible to all social classes, regardless of gender or status
incorrect: Exclusive focus on theological study and strict ritual observance
Restriction of religious practice to political and social elites only
Complete rejection of all traditional religious teachings and practices

questions.text: Architectural syncretism was demonstrated by: (Select all that apply)
correct: Qutub Minar combining Islamic geometric patterns with Hindu temple materials
Great Mosque at Córdoba blending Islamic, Roman, and Spanish architectural elements
Angkor Wat incorporating both Hindu and Buddhist artwork and sculptures
incorrect: Complete rejection of all local architectural traditions in favor of foreign styles
Identical architectural styles developed across all regions simultaneously

Unit_1_Progress_Check_MCQ_Selected_Quest.name: Unit 1 Progress Check: MCQ (Selected Questions and Trick Items)

dar_al_islam.name: Dar al-Islam (Abbasid fragmentation, spread of Islam)

questions.text: The disintegration of the Abbasid Caliphate most directly led to which of the following political developments in the Islamic world in the thirteenth century?
incorrect: The Russian conquest of Central Asia
The conversion of most of the Islamic world to Shi‘a Islam
The collapse of trade along the Silk Road networks

questions.text: Despite the disintegration of the Abbasid Caliphate, Islam continued to spread across Afro-Eurasia in the period 1200–1450 primarily because of which of the following?
incorrect: The conquest of the Christian Crusader States in the Levant
The voyages of the Muslim eunuch Zheng He
The translation activities of Muslim scholars

questions.text: The combination between religious and political aspects in the Abbasid state’s concept of governance is best reflected in which of the following?
correct: The Abbasid state was headed by a caliph who was both the state’s supreme religious and political leader.
incorrect: The Abbasid state had an influential Sunni religious class that often came into conflict with the state.
The Abbasid state often allowed local rulers considerable autonomy.
The Abbasid state patronized the construction of religious buildings such as mosques.

questions.text: Trick: Did the disintegration of the Abbasid Caliphate directly cause the Russian conquest of Central Asia?
correct: No — Russian expansion in Central and Inner Asia began centuries later (early modern period); it was not a direct consequence of Abbasid fragmentation.
incorrect: Yes — it was the immediate result of Abbasid decline.
Yes — it happened simultaneously in the thirteenth century.
The text doesn't say either way.

questions.text: Trick: Did the Abbasid disintegration produce a mass conversion of the Islamic world to Shi'a Islam?
correct: No — although some Shi'a states developed earlier, most of the Islamic world remained Sunni.
incorrect: Yes — disintegration caused mass Shi'a conversion.
Yes — Shi'a rulers replaced Sunni everywhere.
The conversion was complete and immediate.

questions.text: Trick: Did the disintegration of the Abbasid state cause the collapse of Silk Road trade?
correct: No — trade networks remained active; rulers generally protected commerce despite political fragmentation.
incorrect: Yes — Silk Road trade collapsed because of fragmentation.
Trade stopped entirely for the next century.

questions.text: Which institution's adoption by the Abbasids is linked by historians to later fragmentation and the rise of military elites?
incorrect: The universalization of the jizya tax

questions.text: Which of the following best explains why Sufi missionaries were effective at spreading Islam in new regions between 1200–1450? (Select all that apply)
correct: They emphasized mystical, personal experiences accessible to many social classes
They often adapted local customs into Islamic practice
They traveled along trade routes and lived within communities
incorrect: They required converts to immediately abandon all local traditions
They enforced conversion through state military forces

questions.text: The House of Wisdom in Baghdad most directly contributed to which of the following historical developments?
correct: Preservation and transmission of Greek philosophical and scientific texts and fostering original Islamic scholarship
incorrect: Immediate spread of Islam into East Asia
The establishment of universal Sharia courts across Europe
Mass conversion of non-Muslim merchants on the Silk Road

questions.text: Nasir al-Din al-Tusi's mathematical work particularly advanced which field that later influenced European astronomy?
Trick: Did the translation movement in the Islamic world directly result in mass religious conversions to Islam?
correct: No — translation preserved and transmitted knowledge but was not a primary cause of religious conversion.
incorrect: Yes — translated books converted readers to Islam en masse.
Yes — translation campaigns were missionary in purpose.
The translations were only religious texts intended to convert non-Muslims.

questions.text: Which of the following was a common continuity between Abbasid-era governance and the Turkic successor states? (Select all that apply)
correct: Support for Islamic scholarship, art, and architecture
Implementation of Sharia law as a legal foundation
Maintenance of trade networks across Dar al-Islam
incorrect: Complete ethnic Arab leadership across all successor states
Direct administration from Baghdad in all provinces

questions.text: Islamic achievements in medicine during the medieval period included: (Select all that apply)
correct: Comprehensive medical encyclopedias like those by Ibn Sina (Avicenna)
Development of hospitals as centers of medical learning and treatment
Advanced surgical techniques and specialized instruments
incorrect: Discovery of germ theory and antibiotics

south_southeast_asia.name: South & Southeast Asia (Adityawarman inscription and religious context)

questions.text: Which of the following best describes a claim made in the first paragraph of the Adityawarman inscription (Malayapura, c. 1350)?
correct: Statues of Buddhist divine figures could spiritually benefit everyone.
incorrect: King Adityawarman was a Buddhist teacher.
Statues of Buddhist teachers were revered by rulers and common people.

questions.text: The claim in the second paragraph that the king has become "free from all physical desire" can best be understood to mean that:
correct: In Vedic religions, rejecting worldly concerns in order to attain spiritual perfection was considered a virtue.
incorrect: Southeast Asian states’ ruling elites were merely very wealthy.
Buddhist monks and nuns in Southeast Asian societies were required to maintain an ascetic lifestyle (this describes monastics, not the king).
It primarily reflects Indian caste roles.

questions.text: Which of the following is an argument in the second paragraph of the inscription regarding King Adityawarman?
correct: His spiritual attributes and worldly achievements made him a powerful and legitimate ruler.
incorrect: His attention to the welfare of all subjects made him a caring and legitimate ruler (not explicitly stated).
His conquest of all surrounding kingdoms made him legitimate (not asserted).
His knowledge of ships made him effective (not mentioned).

questions.text: Trick: Does the phrase "free from all physical desire" primarily mean the king was simply wealthy?
correct: No — in context it praises religious/spiritual purity (a Vedic/Buddhist virtue), not simply wealth.
incorrect: Yes — it refers to political power only.
The inscription is ambiguous and cannot be interpreted.

questions.text: The Bhakti Movement in India is comparable to Sufism in that both:
correct: Emphasized personal mystical devotion accessible to all social classes.
incorrect: Required conversion to a single monastic lifestyle.
Focused primarily on legalistic religious scholarship.

questions.text: The Qutub Minar in Delhi best represents which historical process?
correct: Architectural syncretism combining Islamic styles with local Indian materials and motifs.
incorrect: An exact copy of Arabian mosque architecture with no local influence.
A purely Hindu temple unchanged since antiquity.
A Christian structure adapted for Islamic use.

questions.text: Trick: Was the jizya tax in the Delhi Sultanate a voluntary donation for charitable institutions?
correct: No — the jizya was a tax on non-Muslims that could create resentment though it allowed continued religious practice.
incorrect: Yes — it applied equally to Muslims and non-Muslims.
No — it was a religious tithe collected by churches.

questions.text: Which piece of evidence most strongly supports Demarest's conclusion about the importance of exotic goods to the Maya region’s economy?
correct: Exotic goods were exchanged over long distances within the Maya lowlands.
incorrect: Exotic goods were traded between the Maya city-states and neighboring states (not the focus here).
The exchange went hand in hand with the exchange of information (supportive but secondary).
The Maya region depended on exotic goods because of low agricultural production.

questions.text: The author directly uses all of the following pieces of evidence to support his argument about exotic goods and the power of Maya rulers EXCEPT that Maya rulers:
correct: Prohibited nobles and priests from wearing certain types of exotic goods reserved for rulers.
incorrect: Used exotic goods in rituals expected by subjects.
Used exotic goods in royal coronations.
Received exotic goods as tribute from vassals.

questions.text: Which of the following does the author cite most directly to support his argument in the third paragraph about the importance of exotic goods in the Maya region?
correct: The existence of unified patterns of behavior in warfare, science, and religion.
incorrect: Emergence of a unified Maya kingdom based on new political models.
New writing systems based directly on earlier Mesoamerican systems.

questions.text: Trick: Did Demarest claim exotic goods created a single unified Maya kingdom?
correct: No — he argued exotic goods helped unify patterns of behavior across the region, not that a single unified kingdom emerged.
incorrect: Yes — he explicitly said a single kingdom formed.
Yes — he described direct political unification.
The passage says nothing about political organization.

questions.text: Which of the following items mentioned by Demarest were commonly exchanged among Maya elites? (Select all that apply)
Trick: Did Demarest assert that exotic goods were exchanged primarily because the Maya had low agricultural output?
correct: No — he emphasized elite demand for exotic goods to maintain status and ritual functions, not low agricultural productivity.
incorrect: Yes — low agriculture was the main reason.
He argued agriculture had collapsed forcing trade.
He said trade replaced agriculture entirely.

africa_ethiopia_mali.name: Africa (Ranzano on Ethiopian embassy; Mali & other items)

questions.text: Which of the following was a continuity in the development of African states in the period circa 1200–1450?
correct: Despite geographical barriers, some African states were able to maintain diplomatic and cultural contacts with the broader Afro-Eurasian world.
incorrect: African states such as Ethiopia maintained close contacts with South and East Asian states but rarely had contacts with Europe.
Most African states rejected traditional forms of international diplomacy.
African states needed to maintain friendly diplomatic relations with Europe in order to expand their empires.

questions.text: Which continuity in African states most likely explains the prevalence of Christianity in Ethiopia?
correct: Some African states’ religious traditions continued to be influenced by cultural transfers dating back to earlier centuries.
incorrect: Religious toleration in general (that alone doesn't explain Ethiopia's Christian prevalence).
African official religions were typically syncretic mixtures (not the specific reason for Ethiopia).
Elites adopted monotheism while the masses remained entirely indigenous (too generalized).

questions.text: Which of the following was a key similarity in the policies of rulers of African states such as Ethiopia in the period circa 1200–1450?
correct: They used religion and laws to bolster their legitimacy.
incorrect: They granted their subjects unrestricted freedom of speech.
They faced uniform challenges from merchant elites across all states.
They sought economic self-sufficiency by restricting trade contacts with other societies.

questions.text: Trick: Did the Ethiopian embassy account indicate Ethiopia had no contacts with Europe?
correct: No — the embassy itself demonstrates Ethiopia maintained diplomatic and cultural contacts with Europe.
incorrect: Yes — Ethiopia was completely isolated from Europe.
It implies only Indian Ocean contacts, not Mediterranean.
The embassy was a fictional story with no diplomatic basis.

questions.text: Mansa Musa's pilgrimage to Mecca is best known historically for:
correct: Demonstrating Mali's immense wealth and increasing West Africa's visibility in Afro-Eurasian maps.
incorrect: Causing the immediate unification of West Africa under Mali rule.
Leading to the permanent colonization of Mali by Middle Eastern states.
Being the first ever Islamic hajj in history.

questions.text: Great Zimbabwe's economic strength was based primarily on:
correct: Control of gold trade and taxation of gold transport to coastal Swahili trading cities.
incorrect: Large-scale manufacturing of textiles for international export.
Extensive maritime naval power in the Indian Ocean.

questions.text: Trick: Did Ibn Battuta describe Kilwa as totally isolated from international trade?
correct: No — he described Kilwa as an active trading city with fine architecture and access to gold from the interior.
incorrect: It was only a small fishing village.
It traded exclusively with China and no one else.

questions.text: The terminology used in the opening sentence of Edward III's decree ("I, Edward, by the grace of God...") was most directly influenced by which of the following?
correct: The deep influence of Christianity on medieval society and culture
incorrect: The notion that the Pope is the supreme leader of the Church
The medieval rules of chivalry and courtly etiquette
The slow and uncertain nature of communications in medieval Europe

questions.text: The process of "consultations with the nobles and clergy of the realm" in the making of Edward III’s decree best reflects which of the following?
correct: The fragmented, decentralized nature of European feudal monarchies
incorrect: The emergence of the concept that parliaments had ultimate authority rather than kings
The legacy of Roman imperial political traditions on medieval European states
European rulers’ tactic of making harsh legislation palatable by claiming it was part of God's will

questions.text: The need for legal action to address the "grave inconveniences" from labor shortages described in the Statute of Laborers most likely resulted from:
correct: The agrarian nature of medieval European economies and their reliance on coerced labor
incorrect: The inability of nobles to take direct legal action
The high social rank and economic standing of ploughmen
Guild regulations capping agricultural labor hours

questions.text: Trick: Does Edward III’s decree indicate medieval kings didn’t need to consult nobles and clergy?
correct: No — the decree states it was made after consultation, reflecting the monarch's need to work with nobles and clergy in practice.
incorrect: No — the process is evidence of parliamentary supremacy.
The decree was a private royal order with no consultation.

questions.text: The Statute of Laborers (1351) most directly attempted to address which post-plague economic problem?
correct: Wage inflation and labor shortages resulting from the Black Death
incorrect: A deliberate policy to eliminate serfdom immediately
A reduction in noble landholdings across England
Restoration of trade with continental Europe

questions.text: Trick: Did the Statute of Laborers give serfs complete freedom to leave their lords?
correct: No — it aimed to bind laborers to previous wage levels and allowed lords to retain serfs.
incorrect: Yes — it granted full mobility to all peasants.

Leading major naval expeditions to Southeast Asia

The city of Timbuktu in the Mali Empire was famous primarily for...

incorrect: Being a major Indian Ocean port

questions.text: In medieval Europe, what was the decentralized political arrangement where lords granted land to vassals in exchange for loyalty and military service called?
What was the system of economic and social relations between landlords and their peasant laborers on self-sufficient agricultural estates in medieval Europe?
The primary form of coerced labor on manors in medieval Europe was...
Which institution held the most political and social power in Western Europe after the fall of the Western Roman Empire, providing order and unity?
What were the Crusades?
correct: A series of religious wars initiated by the Latin Church to reclaim the Holy Land from Muslim control.
incorrect: A series of trade missions to establish new routes to China.
A conflict between the Byzantine Empire and the Russian states.
Peasant revolts against the feudal system.

questions.text: A major long-term consequence of the Crusades for Europe was...
correct: Increased access to Asian luxury goods and the flow of Muslim scholarship into Europe.
incorrect: The permanent conquest of the Middle East by European powers.
The unification of Roman Catholicism and Eastern Orthodoxy.
The complete collapse of the feudal system.

questions.text: The Byzantine Empire was the continuation of which classical civilization?
What was the capital of the Byzantine Empire, known for its strategic location connecting Europe and Asia?
The term 'Caesaropapism' in the Byzantine Empire refers to...
correct: The emperor holding authority over both the state and the church.
incorrect: The Pope having supreme authority over all emperors and kings.
A system of democratic elections for the patriarch.
The separation of church and state.

questions.text: The Great Schism of 1054 was the formal split between which two branches of Christianity?
correct: The Roman Catholic Church and the Eastern Orthodox Church

questions.text: Emperor Justinian's most lasting contribution was...
correct: The codification of Roman law, known as the Corpus Juris Civilis.
incorrect: The permanent reconquest of the Western Roman Empire.
The construction of the Great Wall of Constantinople.
The defeat of the Ottoman Empire.

questions.text: The Byzantine Empire fell in 1453 after Constantinople was conquered by the...
In the period 1200-1450, states in sub-Saharan Africa, such as the Mali Empire, derived significant power and wealth from...
correct: Controlling trans-regional trade routes, particularly the salt and gold trade.
incorrect: Industrial manufacturing of textiles and iron.
Building large naval fleets to conquer overseas territories.
Maintaining isolation from the rest of Afro-Eurasia.

questions.text: The pilgrimage to Mecca by which ruler of the Mali Empire famously displayed the kingdom's immense wealth?
A 'khanate' or 'khaganate' was a polity typically found in which type of society?
correct: Nomadic Mongolic and Turkic societies on the Eurasian Steppe

questions.text: The term 'Pax Mongolica' describes...
correct: A period of stability and safe commercial exchange across Eurasia under Mongol rule.
incorrect: A peace treaty signed between the Mongols and European kingdoms.
The Mongol law code that forbade all forms of trade.
A period of intense civil war among the Mongol successor states.

questions.text: What was the name of the Mongol law code that promoted order through religious tolerance and severe punishment for crimes like theft?
The Mongol Empire's sophisticated communication network, which used a series of relay stations, was called the...
Which of the following was NOT one of the four major successor khanates of the Mongol Empire?
What major global event contributed to the decline of the Pax Mongolica by devastating populations and disrupting trade?
Which of the following are examples of how agriculture shaped social organization in feudal Europe? (Select all that apply)
correct: The vast majority of the population were peasants or serfs tied to the land.
A warrior elite of landowning lords exercised political and military power.
Society was organized around self-sufficient manors.
incorrect: A large, urban-based merchant class was the most powerful group.
All land was owned and worked collectively by free citizens.

questions.text: Which statements accurately describe the differences between Roman Catholicism and Eastern Orthodoxy around 1200 CE? (Select all that apply)
correct: The Roman Catholic Church used Latin, while the Eastern Orthodox Church used Greek.
The Pope was the supreme authority in the Roman Catholic Church, a claim rejected by the Eastern Orthodox Church.
Catholic priests were expected to be celibate, while Orthodox priests could marry.
incorrect: The Orthodox Church was polytheistic, while the Catholic Church was monotheistic.
The Catholic Church was based in Constantinople.

questions.text: How did the Mongol conquests affect the Silk Road?
correct: They unified the territory, making the trade routes safer and leading to increased commercial and cultural exchange.
incorrect: They permanently destroyed the routes and ended all overland trade.
They heavily taxed the routes, making them unprofitable for merchants.
They restricted the routes for military use only.

questions.text: The architectural masterpiece Hagia Sophia was built in Constantinople under the rule of which Byzantine emperor?
In medieval Europe, associations of craftsmen or merchants formed to protect their interests and maintain standards were called...
The expansion of the Mongol Empire facilitated the exchange of which of the following? (Select all that apply)
correct: Gunpowder from China to the West
The Black Death from Asia to Europe
New methods of banking and credit
incorrect: Potatoes and corn from the Americas to Afro-Eurasia

questions.text: Which of these powerful states existed in Sub-Saharan Africa during the 1200-1450 period?
What was a primary cause of political decentralization in Europe following the collapse of the Roman Empire?
correct: Frequent invasions from groups like the Vikings and Magyars disrupted centralized rule.
incorrect: The widespread adoption of democratic ideals.
The Catholic Church's policy of dividing kingdoms.
A long period of peace and prosperity that made armies unnecessary.

questions.text: The Mongol successor state that ruled over Russia was known as the...
The Mongol-ruled dynasty in China, founded by Kublai Khan, was known as the...
The Ilkhanate, a Mongol successor state, was primarily based in which region?
Which statement best describes the role of women in feudal European society?
correct: Women were largely patriarchal, but could exercise some power in convents or within domestic roles.
incorrect: Women held equal political and military power to men.
Women were the primary merchants involved in long-distance trade.
The society was matriarchal, with property and titles passed through the female line.

questions.text: Which two values were central to the debate about Islamic Civilization between 1200-1450?
correct: Trade & Networks of Exchange and Education

questions.text: Which of the following technologies were important agricultural improvements in medieval Europe?
correct: The three-field system and the heavy plow (moldboard)
incorrect: The steam engine and the cotton gin
The magnetic compass and the astrolabe

questions.text: Unlike the fragmented political landscape of Western Europe, the Byzantine Empire was characterized by...
correct: A highly centralized political authority in a single emperor.
incorrect: A system of competing feudal lords.

questions.text: The gold coin of the Byzantine Empire, which became a standard currency in the Mediterranean for centuries, was called the...
The Fourth Crusade (1202-1204) had what disastrous consequence for the Byzantine Empire?
correct: The crusaders sacked and captured Constantinople, severely weakening the empire.
incorrect: It led to a successful alliance between the Byzantines and the Turks.
It resulted in the reconquest of Jerusalem by Christian forces.
It caused the final split between the Catholic and Orthodox churches.

questions.text: Which of these factors contributed to the end of the Early Middle Ages and the start of the High Middle Ages in Europe around 1000 CE?
correct: The cessation of Viking, Magyar, and Muslim invasions.
incorrect: The invention of the printing press.
The beginning of the Protestant Reformation.

questions.text: What was the Mongol attitude toward the different religions of the peoples they conquered?
correct: They practiced religious tolerance, allowing conquered peoples to keep their faiths.
incorrect: They forced all conquered peoples to convert to their shamanistic beliefs.
They adopted and enforced Nestorian Christianity across their empire.
They systematically destroyed all religious sites except for those of their native religion.

questions.text: Though sub-Saharan Africa participated in the Afro-Eurasian economy, its integration was somewhat limited by geographical factors such as...
correct: The Sahara Desert and a lack of natural harbors.
incorrect: The presence of impassable mountain ranges along all coasts.

questions.text: How did rulers in the Mongol Empire incorporate foreign peoples into their society?
correct: They often relocated skilled engineers and artisans to different parts of the empire.
incorrect: They strictly prohibited any interaction between Mongols and conquered peoples.
They enslaved all conquered populations for agricultural labor.
They only accepted people who voluntarily converted to their religion.

questions.text: A significant factor contributing to the flourishing of the Silk Roads in the 13th and 14th centuries was...
correct: the Mongol Empire, which provided stability and security along trade routes (Pax Mongolica)
incorrect: the widespread adoption of Christianity throughout East Asia
the complete collapse of all major empires across Asia
the invention of aircraft, which made long-distance transport faster and safer

questions.text: Which statement best describes the Mongols' attitude toward different religions within their empire?
correct: They were generally tolerant of different faiths as long as conquered peoples remained obedient and paid tribute
incorrect: They forcibly converted all conquered peoples to their native shamanic religious practices
They adopted and enforced Nestorian Christianity as the official state religion throughout the empire
They systematically persecuted all religions, especially Islam and Buddhism

questions.text: Technological innovations like the lateen sail, stern rudder, and astrolabe were crucial for Indian Ocean trade growth because they...
correct: helped sailors navigate more accurately and harness predictable monsoon wind patterns
incorrect: were invented by European explorers like Vasco da Gama during their voyages
allowed ships to travel overland between different seas and ocean systems
were used exclusively by Chinese treasure fleets under Admiral Zheng He

questions.text: The introduction and increased use of which animal was a turning point that massively expanded Trans-Saharan trade?
The travels of Ibn Battuta and Marco Polo are historically significant because they...
correct: provide valuable first-hand accounts of the interconnectedness and diversity of the Afro-Eurasian world
incorrect: prove that the Americas were widely known to Afro-Eurasians before Columbus's voyages
are the only two surviving travelogues from this entire time period
led directly to the establishment and expansion of the Mongol Empire

questions.text: The Black Death (bubonic plague) spread from Asia to Europe in the 14th century primarily along...
correct: established trade routes, carried by merchants and Mongol armies
incorrect: natural bird migration patterns across continents
purposeful biological warfare conducted by the Song Dynasty against enemies
trans-Atlantic voyages from the Americas to Europe

questions.text: Which factors contributed to the expansion of trade networks between 1200 and 1450? (Select all that apply)
correct: Imperial expansion by new states and empires
Increased demand for luxury goods in Europe and Africa
incorrect: The invention of the steam engine

questions.text: What was the **most significant impact** of the Mongol Empire on the Silk Roads trade routes?
correct: The unification of fragmented territories, increased security, and enforcement of laws that benefited merchants.
incorrect: The introduction of Islam to the Silk Roads.
The invention of the magnetic compass.
The development of the first banking houses in Asia.

questions.text: What event is identified as the cause for the **first golden age** of the Silk Roads coming to an end?
correct: The collapse of classical civilizations such as the Roman and Han empires.
incorrect: The start of the bubonic plague.
The Mongol conquest of the Abbasid Caliphate.
The rise of the Ottoman Turks.

questions.text: Which civilizations were primarily responsible for the revival of the Silk Roads, marking its **second golden age** in the 8th and 9th centuries?
correct: Arab merchants from the Abbasid Empire and Tang China.
incorrect: The Byzantine Empire and the Kievan Rus.
The Holy Roman Empire and the Hanseatic League.
The Mongol Empire and the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.text: How did the Crusades contribute to the later expansion of interregional trade networks?
correct: They exposed Europeans to Eastern luxury goods, creating a lasting consumer demand.
incorrect: They established permanent European military garrisons in China.
They invented the caravanserai and flying cash systems.
They convinced the Ottoman Turks to allow free passage of goods.

questions.text: What Chinese naval technologies, developed centuries before this period, helped China control sea-based trade? (Select all that apply)
What was the primary function of dividing the hull of a Chinese **junk** into compartments?
correct: To strengthen the ship for rough voyages and make sinking less likely.
incorrect: To separate different types of cargo for taxation.
To house different classes of sailors.
To allow for faster turning and navigation.

questions.text: What innovation in transportation technology aided overland Silk Road travel by increasing the load animals could carry?
correct: The design of specialized saddles for camels.
incorrect: The invention of the wheeled cart.

questions.text: The city of **Kashgar** became a major center for trade due to what crucial geographic feature?
correct: It was watered by the Kashgar River, providing a source of water and fertile land for food crops.
incorrect: Its strategic location directly on the Mediterranean Sea.
It was the capital of the Mongol Empire.
It had the only gold mines in the region.

questions.text: What evidence of cultural exchange was found in the major trading city of **Samarkand**?
correct: Archaeological remains showing the presence of diverse religions, including Christianity, Buddhism, Zoroastrianism, and Islam.
incorrect: A large, centralized bank that served all of Eurasia.
The first factory for mass-producing porcelain.
A unified government system combining Chinese and Persian law.

questions.text: The **caravanserai** were inns along the Silk Roads that were typically spaced about 100 miles apart. What explains this specific distance?
correct: It was the distance camels could travel before they needed water.
incorrect: It was a day's journey for a loaded cart.
It was the average distance between major rivers.
It was the maximum territory a Mongol patrol could cover.

questions.text: The development of **flying cash** in China was a response to which challenge of increased trade?
correct: The copper coins used in the money economy were too heavy and unwieldy to transport for increasing transaction volumes.
incorrect: The Mongol Empire demanded a new currency for taxes.
The government ran out of copper to mint new coins.
The lack of a standardized system of weights and measures.

questions.text: The Chinese system of **flying cash** served as the model for which later European commercial innovation?
correct: Banking houses established in European cities in the 1300s.
incorrect: The invention of the stock market.
The establishment of the guild system.

questions.text: A **bill of exchange** is a commercial innovation that provided what benefit to merchants?
correct: It acted as a document legally promising payment of a set amount on a set date, encouraging and supporting trade.
incorrect: It allowed merchants to barter goods without using any currency.
It was a receipt for a camel purchased at a caravanserai.
It was a permit required to cross Mongol territory.

questions.text: What is the key characteristic of a **money economy**?
correct: Using a standardized, agreed-upon medium of exchange (money) rather than bartering with commodities.
incorrect: An economy based solely on the exchange of precious metals.
An economy managed by a single, powerful banking house.
An economy where all transactions are recorded in paper form.

questions.text: The Italian merchant Francesco Balducci Peglotti's quote suggests that trade on the Silk Roads in the 14th century required merchants to engage in which practice?
correct: Paying bribes ('a present') to customhouse officers to receive favorable treatment and lower appraisals.
incorrect: Strictly following all published tax laws and tariffs.
Refusing to interact with local government officials.
Only trading through established government-run banks.

questions.text: What was the primary focus and achievement of the **Hanseatic League**?
correct: A commercial alliance that monopolized trade in the North Sea and Baltic Sea, driving out pirates.
incorrect: A military alliance that defeated the Ottoman Empire.
The first European bank to use 'flying cash.'
A confederation of cities along the Mediterranean Sea.

questions.text: The decline of the Hanseatic League by the mid-17th century was primarily due to which political development?
correct: National governments becoming strong enough to protect their own merchants and trade interests.
incorrect: The completion of the Trans-Saharan trade routes.
The depletion of silver and gold in Europe.
The invention of the Chinese junk.

questions.text: The increased production of iron and steel manufactured in China, motivated by increased demand for exports, is an example of what economic process?
What were the primary luxury goods that China exported along the Silk Roads in this period? (Select all that apply)
What were some luxury goods China imported from other parts of Asia? (Select all that apply)
The text suggests that Europe's primary export to China, driven by Chinese eagerness, was:
According to the table of innovations, which commercial innovation originated in the Persian Empire?
Which of the following Chinese inventions, crucial to trade, diffused from China to other parts of Eurasia?
The fact that the Hanseatic League and the Mongol Empire both sought to increase the safety and volume of trade suggests a historical continuity concerning the role of:
correct: Political or commercial authorities providing security to facilitate long-distance exchange.
incorrect: The universal adoption of a single currency.
The rejection of all centralized government control.
The dominance of European naval power.

questions.text: The Italian merchant Francesco Balducci Peglotti (1471) advised treating customs officers with respect and providing 'a present' primarily to achieve what outcome?
correct: To get them to appraise his wares below their real value, reducing taxes.
incorrect: To ensure his goods were transported quickly across the desert.
To guarantee a military escort to the next city.
To receive a loan from a banking house.

questions.text: What were the specific Chinese innovations offered by Tang China that contributed to the newly revived global trade network? (Select all that apply)
The Mongols increased the safety of travel on the Silk Roads by specifically employing which two methods?
incorrect: Building walls along the entire route
Mandating the use of the magnetic compass
Establishing a single currency for all transactions

questions.text: The Chinese **junk**, known for its multiple sails and divided hull, was similar in function and design to which Southwest Asian ship?
Besides water and food, what were the specific handicrafts produced by artisans in the thriving Silk Road city of **Kashgar**?
Besides its religious diversity, what was the Central Asian trading city of **Samarkand** known for? (Select all that apply)
incorrect: Its exclusive control over the gold trade

questions.text: The **Hanseatic League** primarily monopolized trade in which specific goods in the North Sea and the Baltic Sea? (Select all that apply)
To acquire valuable goods from Arab caravans, where did Hanseatic League ships typically travel to after rounding the Atlantic Coast of Western Europe?
correct: The ports of the Mediterranean Sea
incorrect: The ports of the South China Sea
The ports of the Indian Ocean
The ports of the Black Sea

questions.text: Based on the table of innovations, which financial instrument is chronologically the oldest and where did it originate?
correct: Coin, originating in Lydia, Turkey (c. 500 B.C.E.)
incorrect: Bill of Exchange, originating in China (c. 700 C.E.)
Caravanserai, originating in the Persian Empire (c. 500 B.C.E.)
Banking House, originating in China (c. 200 B.C.E.)

questions.text: What Chinese luxury goods was Europe growing 'more eager than ever for' in the 14th and 15th centuries? (Select all that apply)
Under which thematic category does the key term **flying cash** belong?
The fact that Caravanserai were developed in the Persian Empire while Paper Money was developed in China best illustrates which concept?
correct: Financial and commercial innovations were often regional but spread across the broader trade network.
incorrect: The Persian Empire and China controlled all trade in the 14th century.
Europe did not have any commercial innovations in this era.
All trade innovations occurred before the collapse of the Roman Empire.

questions.text: The city of **Samarkand** sits in which river valley, which made it a critical agricultural and stopping point?
Which political factor most enhanced trade security between Europe and Cathay (China) in the 14th and 15th centuries, as suggested by Pegolotti?
correct: The security and unification established by the Mongol Empire.
incorrect: The military strength of the Hanseatic League.
The construction of a unified Great Wall.
The invention of the magnetic compass.

questions.text: Which historical development is considered the turning point leading to the expansion of trade between Asia and Europe in this era?
correct: The conquests by Mongols, which led to improved trade routes and reduced trading costs.
incorrect: The formation of the Silk Roads by Marco Polo.
The invention of silk cloth during early Mongol rule.
The invention of the printing press.

questions.text: Which statement accurately describes the flow of goods and ideas between Europe and China along the Silk Roads in this period?
correct: Silver, gems, and gold went from Europe to China; silk, paper, and gunpowder went from China to Europe.
incorrect: Silk, paper, and Christianity went from Europe to China; gunpowder, silver, and Buddhism went from China to Europe.
Wool and horses went from Europe to China; silver and gold went from China to Europe.

questions.text: Based on Ruy González de Clavijo's account (1402-1406), what element of the social structure in Tabriz represented a continuity with earlier periods in history?
correct: The concealment of women in public using a white sheet and a black mask.
incorrect: The presence of a free and open market where all goods were sold.
The emphasis on beautifully adorned mosques and Islamic scholarship.
The widespread use of paper money for all transactions.

questions.text: Ruy González de Clavijo's description of Tabriz's fine roadways, great buildings, and 'beautifully adorned' Mosques suggests what attitude toward the city's society and culture?
correct: An attitude of admiration and respect for the city's infrastructure, commerce, and culture.
incorrect: An attitude of suspicion regarding the safety and security of the trade routes.
An attitude of disdain for the lack of European-style commercial practices.
An attitude of indifference toward the city's religious practices.

questions.text: How did the establishment of **caravanserai** specifically affect interregional trade in luxury goods during the period 1200–1450?
correct: They provided institutionalized rest stops, security, and animal exchange, enabling safer, continuous long-distance overland travel.
incorrect: They were the first places where the Bill of Exchange was accepted as currency.
They were the primary centers for shipbuilding and naval technology.
They served as defensive fortifications against the Mongol invasions.

questions.text: Provide one example of cultural diffusion or spread between the East and West facilitated by the Silk Roads.
correct: The spread of religious beliefs like Islam to Central Asian cities such as Samarkand and Kashgar.
incorrect: The invention of the astrolabe in Europe.
The adoption of the camel saddle in China.
The use of iron in the Abbasid Empire.

questions.text: Which commercial practice developed in China solved the problem of transporting large amounts of cumbersome copper coins for trade?
correct: Flying cash (a system of credit/paper money deposit and withdrawal).
incorrect: The use of the bill of exchange.
The establishment of the Hanseatic League.

questions.text: The shift in China from bartering to using money meant relying on a medium of exchange with what key characteristic?
incorrect: A value determined by the distance traveled.
A fluctuating value based on the harvest.
A value tied directly to the production of silk.

questions.text: The increased trade with Arab merchants from the Abbasid Empire led China to develop which major innovation in commercial practice?
correct: New financial systems like paper money and flying cash.
incorrect: The use of the magnetic compass.
The building of the Chinese junk.
The establishment of the Hanseatic League.

questions.text: What specific geographic feature allowed cities like Kashgar and Samarkand to become thriving trade centers despite the inhospitable terrain of the Silk Roads?
correct: Access to rivers or water sources that made the surrounding land fertile (oases).
incorrect: Their direct access to the sea via maritime routes.
Their location high in the Himalayan Mountains.
Their vast deposits of iron and steel.

questions.text: The expansion of iron and steel manufacturing in China during this period is evidence of the relationship between trade and which internal economic process?
correct: Proto-industrialization, driven by the increasing demand for Chinese goods.
incorrect: The Great Divergence, driven by European mercantilism.

questions.text: The Mongol nomadic lifestyle primarily consisted of herding which animals, north of the Gobi Desert?
The Mongol culture, shaped by the arid Asian steppes, highly valued which characteristic?
incorrect: The construction of permanent, walled cities.

questions.text: What does the title **Genghis Khan** mean?
What was the **kuriltai**?
correct: A meeting of Mongol chieftains where Temujin was elected Khan.
incorrect: The title given to a Mongol provincial governor.
The religious law code established by Genghis Khan.
A type of siege weapon used against walled cities.

questions.text: In his quest to build power, what was Genghis Khan's primary criterion for appointing people to positions in his growing kingdom?
correct: Personal loyalty, sometimes overriding family ties.
incorrect: Passing the Chinese civil service exam.

questions.text: What city was the capital of the **Jin Empire** (ruled by the Jurchens) that Genghis Khan first attacked in 1210?
The spread of stories detailing Genghis Khan's brutality was a deliberate military strategy that successfully induced what response from some enemy leaders?
incorrect: A call for assistance from Western Europe.

questions.text: Which skill/weapon were Mongolian soldiers particularly proficient with, besides being strong riders?
What was the purpose of the Mongol military strategy known as **feigned defeat**?
correct: To trick enemy forces into pursuing a smaller Mongol band, leading them into an ambush by larger forces.
incorrect: To encourage enemies to join the Mongol army in peace.
To demoralize the Mongols' own troops, testing their loyalty.
To establish an immediate, peaceful surrender agreement.

questions.text: When conquering a settlement, which group of people did Genghis Khan **recruit** for the Mongol Empire, rather than kill?
correct: Those who could read and write

questions.text: The Mongols quickly exploited the expertise of captured engineers from China and Persia to produce which advanced military technologies? (Select all that apply)
incorrect: Cannons (as they were still in development)

questions.text: What was the purpose of Genghis Khan's 'pony express' system?
correct: To keep contact with the far reaches of the empire by having riders carry oral messages.
incorrect: To deliver written letters to Western Europe.
To transport heavy siege weapons across the desert.
To quickly move goods along the Silk Roads for trade.

questions.text: The period of Eurasian history between the 13th and 14th centuries is known as the **Pax Mongolica**, which translates to:
Genghis Khan established the capital of his empire at **Karakorum**. Where is this city located?
correct: Near the center of modern Mongolia.
incorrect: In the mountains of the Jin Empire.

questions.text: Genghis Khan consulted with scholars and engineers of which two traditions in constructing Karakorum and establishing his government?
Which social policy of Genghis Khan was considered liberal for the 13th century and helped inspire loyalty across his diverse empire?
incorrect: The complete banning of all foreign trade.

questions.text: The protection of the Silk Roads by Genghis Khan's soldiers led to which economic period?
correct: The third golden age of the Silk Roads.
incorrect: The first golden age of the Silk Roads.
The decline of the Silk Roads.

questions.text: What was the goal of Genghis Khan's effort to adapt the **Uyghur alphabet**?
correct: To create a single, unified writing system for the entire empire.
incorrect: To record his battles in a new language.
To translate all existing Chinese texts into Mongol.
To establish a unified legal code.

questions.text: The term for a Mongol kingdom is a:
Who led the Mongolian army, known as the **Golden Horde**, into Russia in 1236?
The Golden Horde's conquest of Russia led to the looting and destruction of which major Russian city in 1240?
Why did Batu call off the planned attacks on Italy and Austria in 1241?
correct: The Great Khan's successor, Ogodei Khan, had died, requiring Batu to return for the funeral and succession issues.
incorrect: He was defeated by the French and German knights.
The Bubonic Plague had devastated his troops.
He ran out of supplies and money.

questions.text: The Mongols primarily used a policy of **indirect rule** in Russia. Why did they choose this method?
correct: They preferred not to live in the Russian forests and worked through existing Russian rulers.
incorrect: Russian rulers refused to pay tribute otherwise.
The Golden Horde was too small to govern the region directly.
They wanted to immediately convert the Russian people to Islam.

questions.text: The city-state of **Moscow** benefited from Mongol rule by collecting extra tributes and using the funds for what purpose?
correct: To develop an army and build a coalition to resist the Mongols.
incorrect: To fund the construction of a new Mongol capital.
To pay for the Golden Horde's funeral expenses.
To send wealth directly to Western Europe.

questions.text: The **Battle of Kulikovo** (1380) was a turning point in Russian history because:
correct: The Russian coalition under Moscow's leadership defeated the Golden Horde, leading to a decline in Mongol influence.
incorrect: It was the battle where Batu was killed.
It led to the conversion of all Mongols to Christianity.
It established the first Russian Republic.

questions.text: A long-lasting impact of the Mongol invasions on Russia was the severance of Russia's ties with **Western Europe**, which resulted in what internal development?
correct: Russia developed a more distinctly Russian culture and a foundation for the modern centralized state.
incorrect: Russia adopted the Chinese civil service exam system.
Russia became politically fragmented into smaller states.

questions.text: Who led the Mongol armies into the Abbasid territories, resulting in the destruction of Baghdad in 1258?
The Mongol army of the Il-khanate was finally defeated in 1260 by an alliance between which two groups?
correct: Muslim Mamluks (led by Baibars) and Christian Crusaders in Palestine.
incorrect: The Golden Horde and the Yuan Dynasty.
The Song Dynasty and the Jurchens.
The Hanseatic League and the Byzantine Empire.

questions.text: In the Il-khanate, what role did **Persian** officials and ministers serve in the Mongol administration?
correct: They served as ministers and provincial/local officials to ensure maximum tax collection.
incorrect: They were primarily used as laborers for military tasks.
They served as military leaders in the Mamluk armies.
They were exclusively responsible for shipbuilding.

questions.text: What was the critical turning point in the Il-khanate's religious policy?
correct: Hulegu and most other Mongols in the Il-khanate converted to Islam.
incorrect: They established Christianity as the state religion.
They enforced the use of the Uyghur alphabet.
They outlawed the use of paper money.

questions.text: Which dynasty was ruling China when Kublai Khan began his nearly 40-year conquest of the region?
Kublai Khan established the **Yuan Dynasty**, rebuilding the capital at Zhongdu and renaming it:
How did Kublai Khan specifically alienate the Chinese scholar-gentry class?
correct: Dismantling the civil service exam system.

questions.text: Which secret society began organizing to overthrow the Yuan Dynasty in the 1350s?
The ultimate overthrow of the Yuan Dynasty was led by **Zhu Yuanzhang**, who later founded which Chinese dynasty?
Compared to women in other societies of the time, Mongol women had more independent lives, evidenced by which rights? (Select all that apply)
correct: Tending flocks of sheep and goats.

questions.text: Who conquered the Central Asian territories lost by the shrinking Mongol Empire around the same time the Golden Horde lost its territory?
correct: Tamerlane (also known as Timur the Lame).

questions.text: What is the historical distinction of the Mongol Empire in terms of size?
correct: It was the largest continuous land empire in history.
incorrect: It was the most decentralized empire in history.
It was the shortest-lived empire in history.
It was the first empire to use paper money.

questions.text: During the Pax Mongolica, which specific cultural exchange made the future 'revolution in communication' (printing press) possible?
correct: The transfer of paper-making technology from China to the West.
incorrect: The adoption of the short bow by European armies.
The conversion of all Mongols to Islam.
The use of the magnetic compass for overland travel.

questions.text: Which two specific intellectual contributions were transferred from the Islamic world to Western Europe due to Mongol conquests?
The Mongol conquests played a key role in the transmission of which deadly disease across Eurasia?
correct: The bubonic plague (Black Death) via fleas.

questions.text: The Mongols devised and used a single international law for all their conquered territories. This led to a long-term impact concerning what form of governance in occupied civilizations?
correct: A capacity for centralized power that was continued or copied by successor states.
incorrect: A widespread return to decentralized feudalism.

questions.text: How did Mongol fighting techniques contribute to the end of Western Europe’s use of knights in armor?
correct: The heavily clad knights were too slow and could not react in time to the Mongols' use of speed and surprise.
incorrect: The Mongols killed all the warhorses in Europe.
Mongols refused to fight any opponent wearing metal armor.
Knights were converted and joined the Mongol forces en masse.

questions.text: Against which Mongol military innovation did the era of the walled city in Europe come to an end?
correct: Siege technology (catapults, portable towers, and cannon).

questions.text: Matthew Paris's quote (1240), written from England, is significant because it emphasizes the Mongols' reputation for destruction, which spread **farther than**:
correct: Their actual conquest (he had no firsthand knowledge).

questions.text: According to the Russian Duke's quote, what was the primary motivation of the Mongols that led to their destruction and plundering?
correct: The seizure of wealth and the desire to destroy.
incorrect: A religious mission to convert Christians to Islam.
A desire for honorable, chivalrous warfare.
The long-term goal of establishing a modern, centralized state.

questions.text: A valid counterargument to the Russian Duke's description of the Mongols as purely destructive ('their only object is to plunder, slay, murder, and burn') is that they later:
correct: Established political institutions and ushered in the Pax Mongolica, enforcing laws and protecting trade.
incorrect: Completely withdrew from Russian territory immediately after the conquest.

questions.text: Based on the text, the Mongol reputation for brutality (like the Duke's warning) was an effective tool used to achieve what strategic goal before an invasion, such as against Hungary?
correct: To frighten the targeted population into surrendering without a fight.
incorrect: To attract knights who admired their style of warfare.
To encourage people to hoard goods for a large ransom.
To encourage a temporary alliance between the Christian and Muslim populations.

questions.text: According to the passage regarding Törengene Khatun, which aspect of Mongol leadership continued unchanged during the interim period after the Qa’an's death?
correct: The adherence to established political structures or law (yasas).
incorrect: The immediate convocation of the kuriltai.
The strict prohibition of women in leadership roles.

questions.text: The source on Törengene Khatun demonstrates that Mongol noblewomen, unlike women in Islamic society at the time, could temporarily assume what role, due to their independent status?
correct: Political leadership and regency over the state until a new Khan was appointed.
incorrect: Leading armies in open combat against the Mamluks.

questions.text: The statement 'The Silk Road... was not a road and not made of silk' suggests that the name is a metaphor for what essential function?
correct: A network of highly expansive and connected trade routes across Afro-Eurasia.
incorrect: A single, paved highway designed for the transport of silk.
A system of canals and rivers connecting China to the Mediterranean.
A maritime route that exclusively traded silk products.

questions.text: What two primary, segmented routes constituted the overland portion of the Silk Road network?
correct: Eastern Mediterranean to Central Asia, and Central Asia to China.
incorrect: China to Japan, and India to East Africa.
Rome to India, and the Baltic Sea to the Black Sea.
The Americas to Europe, and Europe to the Middle East.

questions.text: Which Chinese product, produced from the cocoons of mulberry tree-eating worms, was the key export that lent its name to the entire network?
For millennia, the knowledge of silk-making and the techniques for raising the worms were closely guarded secrets in China primarily because:
correct: The lion’s share of China’s wealth came from its silk production.
incorrect: The Emperor used silk solely as a writing material.
The production process required specific weather only found in China.
The Chinese government feared nomadic raiders would steal the worms.

questions.text: How did the volume of goods and the number of merchants compare along the entire length of the Silk Road?
correct: Goods traveled the entire length more than the people, as merchants typically moved goods in segments between towns.
incorrect: Individual merchants traveled the entire route frequently, but goods were often held up at customs points.
The same small group of traders carried the goods from China to Rome and back.
Neither goods nor people traveled much due to continuous warfare.

questions.text: In addition to clothing, what other uses did the Chinese find for silk internally? (Select all that apply)
correct: A means to buy off nomadic raiders
incorrect: A universal currency for all trade

questions.text: What was the primary argument made by the Roman Senate and figures like Seneca the Younger for attempting to ban silk?
correct: Silk purchases caused trade imbalances and the fabric itself was deemed inadequately modest.
incorrect: Silk caused an unknown disease that was spreading through Rome.
The Chinese were using silk to fund military expansion.
Silk interfered with the production of Roman wool and linen.

questions.text: The fact that attempts by the Roman Senate to ban silk failed speaks to the influence of which group in the ancient world?
correct: The wealthy elite, whose consumer demand and wealth shaped governance.
incorrect: The Chinese Emperor, who threatened military action.
The peasant farmers, who relied on silk imports for survival.
The Catholic Church, which endorsed the use of silk.

questions.text: What geographical or environmental factor of Central Asia made the region's nomadic people crucial to Silk Road trade?
