questions.text: Why does John Green mock his 'past self' at the start of the video?
correct: For focusing on trivial details instead of big-picture history

questions.text: What message does the t-shirt example reinforce at the beginning of the episode?
correct: Trade networks create complex global connections between production and consumption
incorrect: Only luxury goods have international supply chains

questions.text: Why does John Green joke about being chased by a red tortoise shell?
correct: He’s referencing a Mario Kart joke tied to Mediterranean exports
incorrect: To warn viewers about real tortoise shell monsters
To show that Romans invented video games

questions.text: What broader historical pattern does the Silk Road illustrate?
correct: Long-distance connections drive cultural, economic, and biological change

Ottoman_Empire_Consolidation_and_Control.name: Ottoman Empire: Consolidation and Control (10 Qs)

questions.text: What term is used to describe the Ottoman practice of abducting Christian boys from the Balkans to serve the state, often called the 'blood tax'?
What was the primary purpose of the Devshirme system within the Ottoman Empire?
correct: To create a loyal, meritocratic class of administrators and elite soldiers directly dependent on the Sultan
incorrect: To punish Christian populations for revolting
To raise money for military campaigns
To quickly convert Christian populations to Islam

questions.text: The recruits of the Devshirme system were rigorously trained and educated to fill two main roles. What were these roles?
correct: Elite military soldiers (Janissaries) and high-ranking government ministers/advisors

questions.text: The highly disciplined and feared elite infantry corps of the Ottoman army, recruited through the Devshirme system, were known as the:
The Ottoman Empire's conquest of Constantinople in 1453 was primarily made possible by which technology?
The conversion of the Hagia Sophia into a mosque is an example of a conqueror asserting which type of legitimacy over a newly acquired territory?
Which title was used by the ruler of the Ottoman Empire?
Which Muslim empires in this period used the new military technology of gunpowder to consolidate their empires (Gunpowder Empires)?
Under **Suleiman the Magnificent**, the Ottoman Empire reached its peak expansion and established its first written code of laws. This demonstrates which method of legitimation?
correct: Monumental Architecture and Codification of Law

questions.text: The system of **Tax Farming** used by the Ottomans led to which negative consequence?
correct: Corrupt local officials who skimmed revenue and exploited peasants
incorrect: Increased loyalty to the central government

questions.text: The Safavid Empire was unique among the Gunpowder Empires because it was the only one that adhered to which branch of Islam?
The main land border conflict between the **Ottoman Empire** and the **Safavid Empire** was caused by what primary factor?
correct: Religious difference (Sunni Ottoman vs. Shi'a Safavid) and control over trade routes

questions.text: The Mughal Empire consolidated power over a large territory that was primarily populated by practitioners of which religion?
The Mughal Emperor **Akbar** earned legitimacy by promoting which policy?
correct: Religious tolerance and the abolition of the Jizya (tax on non-Muslims)
incorrect: Exclusion of all Hindus from government

questions.text: Which piece of monumental architecture in the Mughal Empire is an example of syncretic architecture, blending Islamic and Hindu design elements?
The Mughal Empire's period of greatest religious tolerance and cultural synthesis occurred under the reign of which emperor?
The emperor **Aurangzeb** is known for which policy, which contributed to the decline of the Mughal Empire?
correct: Ending religious tolerance and reimposing the Jizya
incorrect: Forming an alliance with the Safavids

questions.text: The **Zamindars** in the Mughal Empire were a class of people who held which role?
correct: Local tax collectors and landowners who were granted administrative powers
incorrect: Elite infantry soldiers loyal to the emperor

questions.text: What was the capital city of the Safavid Empire, known for its grand mosques and centralized bazaars under Shah Abbas I?
Both the Safavid and Ottoman Empires utilized elite slave soldiers to bolster their centralized military and bureaucracy. Which term describes the general use of this practice?

Russia_China_and_Other_Land_Empires_15_Q.name: Russia, China, and Other Land Empires (15 Qs)

questions.text: Which Russian ruler consolidated power by conquering and assimilating the remnants of the Golden Horde and expanding Russian territory eastward?
The major goal of **Peter the Great**'s reign in Russia was to achieve what?
correct: Westernization and modernization of the military and culture

questions.text: Which new capital city did Peter the Great establish as a 'window to the West'?

The **Qing Dynasty** (1644-1911) was established by people from Manchuria, who maintained power by keeping their distinct ethnic identity separate from the majority Han Chinese. This demonstrates which method of rule?
correct: Maintaining a distinct ethnic/ruling class identity

questions.text: The **Forbidden City** in Beijing, built during the Ming Dynasty, primarily served to demonstrate which form of imperial power?
correct: Monumental architecture to legitimize the ruler's power and symbolize his divine connection
incorrect: A primary trading post for foreign merchants
A democratic meeting place for citizens
A military barracks for the army

questions.text: The Ming Dynasty's policy of re-instituting the **Civil Service Exam** and highly centralized government demonstrated which attempt to consolidate power?
In Russia, the **Oprichnina** established by Ivan IV was a policy of using secret police and terror to control and eliminate which group?
What was a major reason for the expansion of the **Russian Empire** across Siberia and eastward to the Pacific?
incorrect: The search for cotton and silk
The need for a warm-water port in the Mediterranean
The desire to conquer the Safavid Empire

questions.text: Which term describes the economic theory, adopted by many European monarchs, that viewed the world's wealth as finite and sought to maximize exports and accumulate gold and silver?
Which French monarch built the **Palace of Versailles** as a symbol of absolute power and used it to house and control the French nobility?
The **Divine Right of Kings** (used by Louis XIV) and the **Mandate of Heaven** (used by Qing Emperors) are both examples of rulers using which method to legitimize their rule?
The development of a strong, salaried bureaucracy that took power away from regional nobility (such as in France and Russia) is a key characteristic of which form of government?
What was the system of granting land in the Ottoman Empire that allowed the Sultan to reward military service and collect taxes, similar to a feudal system but controlled by the state?
What was a major way the **Qing Dynasty** expanded and maintained its vast territorial control in areas like Tibet and Mongolia?
correct: Utilizing local elites and different administrative techniques for different regions (e.g., Manchu, Mongol, Han)
incorrect: Building a massive navy for coastal defense
Completely converting all populations to Manchu religion

questions.text: What was the term for the tax levied on non-Muslim subjects in Islamic empires like the Ottomans and Mughals, which was abolished by Akbar but later reimposed by Aurangzeb?
Imperial expansion in the period 1450–1750 relied primarily on the increased use of which two military technologies?
Which three empires are historically known as the Islamic 'Gunpowder Empires'?
Which land empire consolidated power in Central and East Asia (China) and maintained a distinct ethnic identity separate from the majority Han Chinese?
Which land empire controlled territory in Southern Europe, the Middle East, and North Africa?
The **Safavid–Mughal conflict** and the **Songhai Empire’s conflict with Morocco** were both driven by what factors?
correct: Political and religious disputes leading to rivalries between states
incorrect: Disputes over control of sea-based trade routes

questions.text: The Battle of Chaldiran (1514) was a decisive conflict that primarily intensified the religious split between which two branches of Islam?
Which consequence did the Moroccan invasion of the Songhai Empire in the 16th century have?
correct: The use of superior Moroccan gunpowder weapons led to the collapse of Songhai.
incorrect: The Moroccans were repelled by the Songhai army.
It led to a new alliance between Morocco and Songhai.
It resulted in the widespread conversion of Moroccan leaders to Animism.

Ottoman_Empire_Consolidation_and_Legitim.name: Ottoman Empire: Consolidation and Legitimacy (10 Qs)

questions.text: The **Devshirme** system ensured the Ottoman government had a loyal corps of bureaucrats and soldiers by doing what?
correct: Abducting Christian boys and training them to be slaves of the Sultan, severing their original loyalties.
incorrect: Recruiting only from the traditional Turkic nobility.
Allowing Christian subjects to freely elect their own leaders.

questions.text: The highly disciplined and feared elite infantry corps of the Ottoman army, recruited via the Devshirme system, were the:
The Ottoman system of **Tax Farming (Iltizam)**, while profitable for the government, had which negative effect?
correct: It led to corruption, as tax farmers reaped up to five times the amount paid and exploited peasants.
incorrect: It provided better social services to the poor.
It stabilized the price of New World silver.
It increased military pay for the Janissaries.

questions.text: The Ottoman conquest of Constantinople in 1453 and the subsequent conversion of the Hagia Sophia into a mosque served what purpose?
correct: To assert religious and political legitimacy over the former Christian Byzantine Empire.
incorrect: To demonstrate the Sultan's religious tolerance.

questions.text: The **Millet System** in the Ottoman Empire demonstrates which policy toward religious and ethnic diversity?
correct: Accommodation: allowing non-Muslim confessional communities to rule themselves under their own personal laws.
incorrect: Suppression: forced conversion of all subjects to Sunni Islam.
Expulsion: forcing all non-Muslims out of the empire.
Assimilation: requiring all subjects to speak only Turkish.

questions.text: The **Topkapi Palace** in Istanbul, which housed the Sultan and the Harem, primarily reinforced and legitimized authority through:
correct: Monumental architecture and the physical centralization of imperial power and bureaucracy.
incorrect: Its democratic meeting halls for citizens.
Its use as a primary trade marketplace.

questions.text: What was a major internal problem that plagued the Ottoman Empire's political stability and led to less competent rulers?
correct: Succession issues: sons often fought each other for the throne, leading to sheltered or unprepared rulers.
incorrect: The lack of an army after 1600.
The absence of any written laws.
The dominance of the peasantry in politics.

questions.text: How did the influx of New World silver negatively impact the Ottoman economy?
correct: It caused inflation, reducing the real value of tax revenues collected at legally fixed rates.
incorrect: It caused massive deflation, making Ottoman goods too expensive.
It led to a massive trade deficit with China.
It funded all European rivals for centuries.

questions.text: The rise of the Janissaries and the reduction of the landholding cavalrymen resulted in which shift in Ottoman power?
correct: A shift in power from the old aristocracy to the new military professionals (Janissaries).
incorrect: A complete end to all military spending.
The rise of female rulers in the military.
A transfer of power to the Christian clergy.

questions.text: The Sultan's role in the Ottoman Empire was a blend of which three identities?
correct: Turkic warrior prince, Muslim caliph, and conquering emperor
incorrect: European monarch, Hindu Brahmin, and Chinese scholar
Shi'a imam, Mongol khan, and tax collector
Naval admiral, peasant farmer, and merchant

Mughal_Empire_Rule_and_Social_Structure_.name: Mughal Empire: Rule and Social Structure (7 Qs)

questions.text: What was the Mughal Empire's most challenging religious and ethnic dynamic?
correct: Muslim leaders ruling over a population that remained primarily Hindu.
incorrect: Buddhist leaders ruling over a population that was primarily Christian.
Sunni leaders ruling over a population that was primarily Shi'a.
A Manchu minority ruling over a Han majority.

questions.text: Mughal Emperor **Akbar**'s policy of religious tolerance, which included abolishing the **Jizya**, was primarily intended to:
correct: Incorporate Hindu subjects into the military and bureaucracy to consolidate loyalty.

questions.text: The **Zamindars** in the Mughal Empire were a class of people who held which role?
correct: Local officials who collected taxes and were often hereditary landowners
incorrect: The elite slave soldiers of the Emperor
The royal astronomers and court poets
The Hindu priests responsible for temple upkeep

questions.text: The construction of the **Taj Mahal** by Shah Jahan is an example of a ruler using which method to legitimize and memorialize their rule?
incorrect: Public works projects for the poor
Military conquest of a new continent

questions.text: Which emperor is known for reversing Akbar's policies, reimposing the **Jizya**, and persecuting Hindus, which contributed to the empire's instability?
The Mughal practice of incorporating **rajas** (regional Hindu leaders) into military and bureaucratic positions is a form of:
correct: Recruitment of bureaucratic elites to maintain centralized control

questions.text: Despite the patriarchal nature of the Mughal Empire, women like **Mumtaz Mahal** (Shah Jahan's wife) and royal wives in other empires maintained power through which means?
correct: Property rights and influence in royal court and politics (e.g., the Sultanate of Women)
incorrect: Serving as generals in the army

Safavid_Empire_Shia_Identity_and_Conflic.name: Safavid Empire: Shi'a Identity and Conflicts (5 Qs)

questions.text: The **Safavid Empire**'s official adherence to **Shi'a Islam** established what long-lasting political and religious dynamic?
correct: It created a distinct, separate identity from the surrounding Sunni-majority states (especially the Ottomans).
incorrect: It led to total peace with the Ottoman Empire.
It allowed them to conquer Europe.
It required them to abolish the tax on all non-Muslims.

questions.text: How did **Shah Abbas I** of the Safavid Empire consolidate military power, similar to the Ottomans?
correct: By capturing and educating boys (from Russia) as slave infantrymen trained to use firearms, weakening the traditional Qizilbash chiefs.
incorrect: By building a massive navy to compete with Europe.
By relying solely on the traditional Turkic warrior elite.
By adopting a policy of pacifism.

questions.text: The **Battle of Chaldiran** demonstrated the military superiority of the **Ottomans** over the **Safavids** primarily because:
correct: The Ottoman Janissaries had superior gunpowder weapons (cannons and muskets) against the Safavid cavalry.
incorrect: The Ottomans outnumbered the Safavids ten to one.
The Safavids used elephants in battle.

questions.text: Why was the Safavid capital, **Isfahan**, less cosmopolitan and had fewer international traders than Istanbul?
correct: Isfahan was an inland city and did not have the access to international sea trade routes that Istanbul controlled.
incorrect: Isfahan was controlled by the Portuguese.
Isfahan was a primary hub for Trans-Saharan trade.
Isfahan was a military barracks, not a trade center.

questions.text: The use of **mullahs** (local mosque officials) supervised by the state in the Safavid Empire is an example of the ruler using which method to legitimize power?
correct: Religious ideas and institutions to support the state

questions.text: The **Qing Dynasty** (Manchu) maintained control over the majority Han Chinese by using which restrictive policy?
correct: Maintaining a distinct ethnic/ruling class identity and limiting certain groups’ roles in society (restrictive policies against Han Chinese).
incorrect: Adopting a policy of total religious tolerance for all religions.
Promoting democratic elections for all subjects.

questions.text: Which practice did the Ming Dynasty re-institute to consolidate power and establish a centralized bureaucracy before the Qing takeover?
The goal of **Peter the Great** in moving the Russian capital to **St. Petersburg** was primarily to achieve:
correct: Westernization and provide a 'window to the West' for naval and cultural modernization.
incorrect: An alliance with the Ottoman Empire.

questions.text: The use of the **Oprichnina** (secret police) by **Ivan IV** in Russia was a method of consolidating power by suppressing which group?
correct: The traditional Russian noble landowning class (Boyars)

questions.text: The **Palace of Versailles** (France) and the **Forbidden City** (China) are both examples of rulers using what to legitimize and centralize their rule?
correct: Monumental architecture to demonstrate wealth and control over the nobility
incorrect: Allowing free trade with all nations

questions.text: The concept of **Divine Right** used by European monarchs (like Louis XIV) and the **Qing Imperial Portraits** are methods used by rulers to:
correct: Legitimize their rule using religious ideas or art
incorrect: Challenge the economic policies of other states

questions.text: All three major Islamic Land-Based Empires (Ottoman, Safavid, Mughal) relied on agriculture for revenue, but were negatively impacted by which global economic event?
correct: Inflation caused by the increasing amount of New World silver
incorrect: A global decrease in the price of spices
A series of massive environmental disasters
The widespread adoption of paper money

questions.text: Which tax-collection system was employed by the **Mughal Empire** to generate revenue?
incorrect: Tax collection in hard currency (paper money)

questions.text: What was the **Ming Dynasty**'s innovative practice for collecting taxes to generate state revenue?
correct: Collecting taxes in hard currency (e.g., silver)

questions.text: The **Mexica** (Aztec) empire's primary method of generating revenue from conquered territories was through:
correct: Tribute lists of goods and labor
incorrect: The collection of taxes in New World silver

questions.text: Despite all three Islamic empires being on the decline by 1750, they all relied on **land grants** (Timar, Jagir) given to aristocracy in exchange for which service?
Which factor largely contributed to the decline of the Islamic Land-Based Empires by 1750?
correct: The growing dominance of sea-based powers and the failure to adapt to new military and economic technologies.
incorrect: The end of all religious conflict.
The sudden collapse of all agricultural production.
Massive territorial gains that were too easy to defend.

Social_Cultural_and_Religious_Shifts_6_Q.name: Social, Cultural, and Religious Shifts (6 Qs)

questions.text: The development of **Sikhism** in South Asia during this period is a major example of what historical process?
correct: Syncretism: Interaction between Hinduism and Islam
incorrect: The end of all caste distinctions

questions.text: The Ottoman Empire's initial failure to adopt the **European printing press** was due to:
correct: Conservative religious leaders who viewed European societies as backwards and suppressed the technology.
incorrect: The Sultan banning all forms of written communication.
The Janissaries controlling all technological development.
A lack of paper in the empire.

questions.text: What was a continuity in the status of women across all three Islamic Land-Based Empires (Ottoman, Safavid, Mughal)?
correct: All three remained patriarchal, but women in the royal court and elite classes could maintain property rights and political influence.
incorrect: All women were forced to join the military.
All women lost their property rights.

questions.text: The use of domes and intricate Persian calligraphy in mosque architecture was a shared cultural characteristic of which two empires?
In the Ottoman social structure, which group was typically organized into **guilds**?
Which statement accurately reflects the change in the status of Turkic women within the Ottoman Empire?
correct: Their rights declined as the empire adopted more restrictive Islamic and urban practices, leading to veiling and seclusion.
incorrect: Their rights increased dramatically as the empire expanded.
Their status remained unchanged throughout the empire's history.
They were forced to convert to Greek Orthodoxy.

questions.text: The fact that **Ismail** (Safavid) sent cavalry armed with swords and knives to fight **Janissaries** (Ottoman) armed with cannons and muskets demonstrates which key concept?
correct: The critical importance of adopting gunpowder technology for success in the 1450-1750 period.
incorrect: The superiority of nomadic cavalry tactics.
The Ottoman reliance on religious devotion for victory.
The Safavid adoption of isolationist policies.

questions.text: The Mughal and Safavid Empires' reliance on inland urban areas and armies, while neglecting navies, primarily meant they were not competing for:
correct: Overseas territories and sea-based trade control (unlike European powers).
incorrect: Control of the major cotton and indigo trade.
Control of their own agricultural production.

questions.text: The statement 'no distinction is attached to birth among the Turks... it is by merit that men rise in the service' (from The Turkish Letters) is a primary justification for which Ottoman policy?
correct: The use of the Devshirme system to create a meritocratic elite bureaucracy.
incorrect: The maintenance of a rigid, hereditary caste system.
The belief in the divine right of the Sultan.

questions.text: The Mughal emperor **Akbar**'s attempt to unite his diverse empire with the new **Divine Faith** (combining Muslim, Hindu, etc.) is an example of which method of legitimation?
correct: Using religious ideas to cement loyalty and promote political unity.
incorrect: Suppressing all non-Muslim beliefs by force.

questions.text: In the period 1450-1750, a major **continuity** in belief systems was:
correct: Various religions (like Islam and Divine Right) continued to be utilized by rulers to justify political authority.
incorrect: The Sunni/Shi'a split was completely resolved.
Islam declined in importance across North Africa and Asia.
All major religions were replaced by secular governments.

questions.text: The expulsion of Jews from Spain and Portugal and their acceptance in the Ottoman Empire demonstrates which historical development?
correct: Varying approaches to accommodating or suppressing ethnic and religious diversity among states.
incorrect: A universal commitment to religious tolerance across the globe.
A universal trend toward expelling all minority groups.
A continuity in Spanish and Portuguese policies.

Ottoman_Empire_Rulers_Systems_and_Declin.name: Ottoman Empire: Rulers, Systems, and Decline (20 Qs)

questions.text: The Ottoman Empire began as a small **Turkic warrior group** that migrated into **Anatolia**, modern-day Turkey.
The Ottoman Empire was named for which founder?
The system of granting land and tax revenue to military service members in exchange for service, often leading to unrest when reduced, was the:
Who was the wife of **Suleiman the Magnificent** whose political influence contributed to the era known as the 'Sultanate of Women'?
The **Sultanate of Women** was a time when the wives and mothers of the Sultan exercised significant **power and influence** over the empire's decisions.
How did the **Topkapi Palace** reinforce and legitimize Ottoman political authority?
correct: By centralizing the Sultan's power in a highly ritualized and architecturally dominant structure (Monumental Architecture)
incorrect: By providing public housing for all peasants
By serving as a primary naval base

questions.text: The **Janissaries** came to control the weapons that ensured the Ottomans' military success, which allowed them to gain what by the mid-16th century?
correct: Political and economic power, including a say in the Sultan's decisions
incorrect: Complete control over all tax collection
A ban on all gunpowder use

questions.text: Why did the Sultan begin to reduce the number of landholding cavalrymen, causing unrest?
correct: The new Janissary elite infantry, using heavy artillery, checked the power of the old cavalry aristocracy.
incorrect: The cavalry became too loyal to the Sultan.
The horses were all killed by disease.

questions.text: Ogier Ghiselin's *The Turkish Letters* describes the Ottoman system as highly meritocratic, where a man's position is marked by **'the duties he discharges.'** This suggests which key development?
correct: A system of bureaucratic elites based on competence rather than birth/wealth.
incorrect: A system based purely on hereditary right.
A system dominated by popular vote.
A system where all power belonged to the Turkic nobility.

questions.text: The **Millet System** is most closely linked to Islamic rules on the treatment of **non-Muslim minorities** living under Islamic dominion, known as:
The Ottoman Empire was referred to as the **'sword of Islam'** because the rulers combined the roles of Turkic warrior prince, Muslim caliph, and conquering emperor, asserting:
The **Suleymaniye Mosque** in Istanbul, with its impressive domes, was designed by whom?
Which factor primarily contributed to the Ottoman Empire falling behind in technological and cultural developments by the 17th century?
correct: Conservative religious leaders who prevented the adoption of the printing press and saw European societies as backwards.
incorrect: A lack of available paper and ink in the empire.
The Sultan personally banning all new technology.
Constant warfare with the Mughal Empire.

questions.text: The **Millet System** allowed non-Muslims to rule themselves under their own personal laws in cases that did not involve:
The high taxes for peasants and corruption among local governments contributed to which domestic problem in the late Ottoman Empire?

Safavid_and_Mughal_Empires_Specifics_20_.name: Safavid and Mughal Empires: Specifics (20 Qs)

questions.text: The Safavid Empire traced its religious authority to which mystical Islamic tradition?
The Safavids, under **Ismail**, used the expansion of their empire as an extension of their faith, believing that defeating the **Sunni** was an act of faith.
The **qizilbash** (Turkish chiefs) in the Safavid Empire were gradually replaced in military power by:
correct: Slave infantrymen trained to use firearms (Ghulam)

questions.text: Which ruler brought in **European advisors** to assist in wars with the Ottomans, specifically to improve cannons and muskets for the Safavid army?
Why did **Shah Abbas I** not build a Safavid navy despite improving the army's size and efficiency?
correct: The Safavid capital (Isfahan) was inland, and the ruler's focus was on land-based military power, neglecting sea-based trade.
incorrect: He ran out of trees for shipbuilding.
He feared a direct naval conflict with the Ottomans.
The Safavids were primarily focused on the Trans-Saharan trade.

questions.text: What was the **signature business** of the Safavid economy, often decorated with brightly colored floral patterns?
The cultural tradition that kept the **Persian identity strong** despite the use of Arabic in other Islamic lands was:
correct: Poetry, history, drama, and fiction in the Persian language
incorrect: The adoption of the Turkish language
The strict adherence to Sunni Islam

questions.text: The Mughal Empire was founded by **Babur**, who was a descendant of which two great conquerors?
incorrect: Peter the Great and Ivan the Terrible

questions.text: Akbar's policy of cooperation with the Hindu majority included **intermarriage** and an end to the ban on the building of:
Akbar's creation of the **Divine Faith** was intended to:
correct: Reconcile the differences between Muslim and Hindu subjects and cement loyalty to the emperor
incorrect: Force all subjects to convert to Shi'a Islam
Fund the construction of new warships

questions.text: The Mughal bureaucracy was modeled on a **military hierarchy** for the purpose of:
correct: Collecting taxes and administering land revenue in exchange for service
incorrect: Maintaining peace with the Safavid Empire

questions.text: The Mughals grew wealthy by controlling commercial networks based on which three key goods?
Mughal Emperor **Aurangzeb**'s decision to rid India of Hinduism and reimpose the Jizya tax contributed to:
correct: Local leaders plotting against him and general political instability of the empire
incorrect: A massive surge in Mughal power and wealth
The spread of the Divine Faith
The decline of the Zamindar system

questions.text: The development of **Sikhism** in South Asia arose from the context of interactions between which two major belief systems?
The Mughal ruler's persecution of the ninth **Guru** of Sikhism led to what **change** in the nature of Sikhism?
correct: It changed from a peaceful spiritual movement to one that was more militant and defensive.
incorrect: It caused Sikhism to disappear from South Asia.
It led to a full conversion of all Sikhs to Islam.
It caused Sikhism to move to Europe.

Songhai_Russia_and_Global_Comparisons_15.name: Songhai, Russia, and Global Comparisons (15 Qs)

questions.text: The **Songhai Empire** gained access to **gold mines** and grew powerful by taking over from the declining:
The capital of the Songhai Empire, which became a major Islamic center of learning and commerce, was:
In the Songhai Empire, **Islam** was popular in the **cities**, while the rural areas maintained their traditional:

correct: Morocco was near bankruptcy and sought the gold mines and wealth of the Songhai Empire.
incorrect: The Moroccan ruler wanted to convert Songhai to Shi'a Islam.

questions.text: What was the decisive factor that allowed the Moroccan army to defeat the much larger Songhai army?
What was the major consequence of the sacking of Gao, Timbuktu, and Djenne by the Moroccans?
correct: The weakening and eventual collapse of the Songhai Empire.
incorrect: The unification of all West African states.

The rise of a Songhai navy.

questions.text: Which Russian ruler consolidated power by conquering and assimilating the remnants of the **Golden Horde**?
What was a major reason for the expansion of the Russian Empire across Siberia to the Pacific?
incorrect: The search for coffee and tea
The need for a Mediterranean naval base
The desire to conquer North America

questions.text: The **Manchu** (Qing) maintained their distinct ethnic identity and control over the Han Chinese by ensuring which group was kept in power?
Which method of legitimation is common to the **Divine Right of Kings** (Europe), the **Mandate of Heaven** (China), and the **Sultan** as 'Caliph' (Ottoman)?
correct: Rulers using religious, divine, or celestial ideologies to justify and reinforce their authority.

questions.text: A key difference between the Ottoman and Safavid/Mughal Empires was that the **Ottoman** Empire possessed a powerful **navy** and controlled water traffic, while the others did not.
Why were the Safavid and Mughal economies generally at a **disadvantage** in the world market by 1750?

LEQ_Skill_Application_using_Unit_3_10_Qs.name: LEQ Skill Application using Unit 3 (10 Qs)

questions.text: An essay analyzing how the **Ottoman Devshirme** system shifted power away from the old aristocracy to the **Janissaries** would primarily employ which historical thinking skill?
An essay comparing the **Mughal Zamindar system** with the **Ottoman Tax Farming (Iltizam)** system would best be structured using which outline?
To earn the **Contextualization** point for an essay on the *Mughal Empire's consolidation of power (1450-1750)*, a student should discuss which pre-1450 development?
correct: The Delhi Sultanate's prior, tenuous control over a Hindu-majority population.
incorrect: The unification of Germany under Otto von Bismarck.
The start of the Russian Revolution.
The discovery of the New World by Columbus.

questions.text: An essay that argues the **continuity** of Muslim rulers using **religious law** (like the **Jizya**) despite the **change** of some rulers (like Akbar) temporarily **abolishing** it would use which historical thinking skill?
What is the core requirement for an LEQ **Thesis Statement**?
correct: Present a historically defensible claim that answers all parts of the prompt.
incorrect: State an obvious fact about the time period.

questions.text: To earn the **Complexity** point, a student should go beyond the prompt by doing what, using the Ottoman and Safavid conflict as an example?
correct: Explaining how the religious rivalry (Sunni/Shi'a) *intensified* the political/military rivalry (land disputes).
incorrect: Simply naming the two empires in the thesis.
Only discussing one cause of the conflict.

questions.text: The use of **Confucianism** to shape the social hierarchy in Japan is an example of which process, common in East Asia?

The phrase 'Gunpowder Empires' refers to the **Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal** Empires. Beyond mere weaponry, what were the *three* major political and social effects of incorporating gunpowder technology on these states?
correct: The shift of power away from nomadic cavalry or decentralized feudal lords to central authority figures.
The requirement for specialized, salaried military professionals, leading to bureaucratic complexity.
The construction of costly, large-scale siege artillery and fortifications, necessitating increased state revenue (taxes).
incorrect: A universal shift from monotheism to polytheism across all three empires.
The immediate elimination of all inter-regional trade routes.
The creation of democratically elected governmental bodies.

questions.text: The **Manchu (Qing) Dynasty** expanded across Central and East Asia. Which *two* policies demonstrate how the Manchu both preserved their distinct identity while legitimizing their rule to the Han Chinese majority?
correct: Adopting the Confucian civil service exam and bureaucracy (to appease Han scholars).
Maintaining the Manchu queue hairstyle for all male subjects (to signify submission/distinct identity).
incorrect: Eliminating all land-based taxes on the Han peasantry.
Converting the entire Chinese population to Manchu shamanism.
Establishing Beijing as the capital city for the first time in Chinese history.

questions.text: Analyze the **Safavid–Mughal conflict** over the city of **Kandahar**. This conflict was not merely about territory but also about control over which crucial geopolitical and economic factor?
correct: Control of the Silk Road mountain passes connecting Persia and India, vital for armed trade.
incorrect: Religious differences between the Sunni Safavids and the Shi’a Mughals.
Access to the key port of Hormuz on the Persian Gulf.
The demand for European silver and gold currency.

questions.text: Compare the **Ottoman Devshirme** system with the Japanese **Salaried Samurai** system. What *shared purpose* did these two distinct methods serve for their respective central governments?
correct: To create a class of professional military and bureaucratic elites who were loyal only to the central ruler, rather than to local power structures or hereditary nobility.
incorrect: To eliminate all private land ownership within the empire.
To solely convert subject populations to the ruler's religion.
To dismantle existing tax collection systems in favor of tribute lists.

questions.text: The European notion of **Divine Right** was utilized to legitimize rule. What *preceding historical shift* (c. 1300-1600) made it necessary for European monarchs to adopt this highly centralized, religiously-based claim to absolute authority?
correct: The weakening of the Roman Catholic Church's political power and the rise of nation-states, requiring a new justification for absolute secular rule.
incorrect: The widespread adoption of Confucianism in Western Europe.
The collapse of the Atlantic trade network.
The invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg.

questions.text: Analyze the **Mughal Zamindar** system and the **Ottoman Tax Farming** system. Despite differences, what *shared negative consequence* did both innovative tax systems eventually have on the respective empires?
correct: Both systems allowed local agents (Zamindars or tax farmers) to accumulate excessive power and wealth by keeping a surplus of collected revenue, leading to corruption and reduced state revenue over time.
incorrect: Both led to the complete abolition of all military expenditure.
Both forced a massive migration of peasants into urban areas to find work.
Both exclusively relied on collecting taxes in precious metals like silver.

questions.text: The construction of the **Mughal mausolea (like the Taj Mahal)** and **European palaces (like Versailles)** served a similar function in legitimizing power. This falls primarily under which of the **SPICE** (Social, Political, Interaction, Cultural, Economic) characteristics?
correct: Political (demonstrating the ruler's immense wealth, power, and prestige to both domestic and international audiences, consolidating central authority).
incorrect: Economic (since they generated little direct income).
Interaction (since they primarily served an internal political function).
Social (as they did not substantially alter the class structure).

questions.text: How did the **Protestant Reformation**'s central theological tenet—the emphasis on individual Bible reading (**Sola Scriptura**) and its promotion of **vernacular printing**—set the stage for the later **Scientific Revolution and Enlightenment**?
correct: It fostered a culture of individual critical inquiry, questioning established authority, and increased literacy across Europe, which were essential precursors to new scientific and philosophical thought.
incorrect: It strictly limited the number of printing presses in Europe to control the spread of new ideas.
It caused the immediate reunification of the Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches.
It led to the adoption of a unified European currency.

questions.text: Explain how the geopolitical rivalry between the **Ottoman (Sunni)** and **Safavid (Shi’a)** empires transformed a religious difference into a *lasting cultural and political division* across the Middle East. **(TEA Format)**
correct: The continuous conflict over territory and trade routes, such as the Ottoman control of Baghdad, turned theological differences into state-sponsored political identities. The Safavids established Shi'a Islam as the state religion of Persia (Iran), permanently differentiating the Iranian cultural sphere from the largely Sunni Ottoman, Mughal, and other Muslim-dominated regions. This institutionalized split became a defining feature of the region's political landscape.
incorrect: The rivalry was solely economic and had no bearing on religious identity after the 17th century.
It led both empires to completely abandon trade with Europe in favor of internal markets.
It resulted in the Safavids converting entirely to Sikhism to find a neutral religious ground.

questions.text: The development of **Sikhism** in South Asia is a powerful example of **syncretism**. What does its emergence *infer* about the cultural and political environment of the **Mughal Empire** in the late 15th to early 17th centuries?
correct: The environment was generally culturally diverse and tolerant enough (especially under early emperors like Akbar) to allow for the peaceful blending of Hindu and Islamic philosophical traditions, although persecution later intensified under Aurangzeb.
incorrect: It infers that the Mughal Empire had completely outlawed both Hinduism and Islam.
It infers that the only major cultural interaction occurring was with European trade companies.
It infers that the Safavid Empire had politically annexed all of South Asia.

Factual_Recall_and_SPICE_Essentials_High.name: Factual Recall and SPICE Essentials (High-Yield Facts)

questions.text: Identify the **Political** characteristic of the **Qing Dynasty** that allowed them to successfully rule over the Han Chinese population while maintaining Manchu elite status.
correct: The dual administration system, where key bureaucratic positions were shared between Manchu and Han officials.
incorrect: Abolishing the use of the Confucian Civil Service Exams.
Mandating the conversion of all Han Chinese to Manchu shamanism.
Using the Devshirme system to recruit soldiers.

questions.text: What was the **Cultural** change initiated by the Mughal Emperor **Akbar** regarding religious policy, which temporarily eased tensions between Hindus and Muslims in the empire?
correct: Abolishing the *jizya* (tax on non-Muslims) and actively promoting religious tolerance.
incorrect: Establishing the Divine Right of Kings as a legitimizing factor.
Adopting Shi'a Islam as the mandatory state religion.
The construction of the Palace of Versailles.

questions.text: Which **Economic** activity was absolutely critical to the **Ottoman Empire's** financial success, both for expansion and maintenance, from the 15th to 17th centuries?
correct: Controlling key trade choke points, including the Bosporus, Dardanelles, and major land routes connecting Europe and Asia.
incorrect: Exclusive reliance on the silver mining of the Potosi mine.
Generating all state income through the sale of silk textiles.

questions.text: The defining **Cultural** characteristic of the **Safavid Empire** that separated it from its major land-based neighbors (Ottomans and Mughals) was its establishment of what religious branch as the official state faith?
For an LEQ on land-based empires (1450-1750), which event provides the **best contextual link** from the preceding period (c. 1200-1450)?
correct: The **Mongol Empire's** creation of a vast, integrated Eurasian network, which, despite its collapse, left behind military and administrative precedents that successor states like the Ottomans and Mughals adapted.
incorrect: The First Punic War between Rome and Carthage.
The Neolithic Revolution and the start of settled agriculture.
The invention of the steam engine in Britain.

incorrect: A universal shift from monotheism to polytheism across all three empires.
The immediate elimination of all inter-regional trade routes.
The creation of democratically elected governmental bodies.

questions.text: The **Manchu (Qing) Dynasty** expanded across Central and East Asia. Which *two* policies demonstrate how the Manchu both preserved their distinct identity while legitimizing their rule to the Han Chinese majority?
correct: Adopting the Confucian civil service exam and bureaucracy (to appease Han scholars).
Maintaining the Manchu queue hairstyle for all male subjects (to signify submission/distinct identity).
incorrect: Eliminating all land-based taxes on the Han peasantry.
Converting the entire Chinese population to Manchu shamanism.
Establishing Beijing as the capital city for the first time in Chinese history.

questions.text: Analyze the **Safavid–Mughal conflict** over the city of **Kandahar**. This conflict was not merely about territory but also about control over which crucial geopolitical and economic factor?
correct: Control of the Silk Road mountain passes connecting Persia and India, vital for armed trade.
incorrect: Religious differences between the Sunni Safavids and the Shi’a Mughals.
Access to the key port of Hormuz on the Persian Gulf.
The demand for European silver and gold currency.

questions.text: The **Songhai Empire’s conflict with Morocco** (1591) resulted in Songhai’s collapse. What technological disparity enabled the Moroccan victory despite Songhai's size and resources?
correct: Morocco’s use of gunpowder weapons (muskets and cannons) against Songhai’s cavalry and traditional weapons.
incorrect: Morocco’s superior navy controlling the Atlantic trade routes.
A massive plague outbreak that only affected the Songhai army.
Songhai's failure to convert to Islam.

questions.text: Beyond military strategy, the continuous expansion of the Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal empires was primarily driven by what **economic necessity** to sustain their expensive, centralized bureaucracies and standing armies?
correct: The need to capture new populations for taxation and control of key trade routes/agricultural lands to generate revenue.
incorrect: The need to find new sources of coal for industrial production.
The mandate from European monarchs to colonize the Americas.
A universal shift to a barter-only economy.

questions.text: While both the Ottoman and Mughal Empires used gunpowder, how did their **primary military focus** differ based on their geographic location?
correct: Ottomans focused heavily on naval power (Mediterranean) and siege warfare (Europe), while Mughals focused primarily on land-based conquest and field battles in South Asia.
incorrect: Mughals used only cannons, while Ottomans used only muskets.
Ottomans exclusively recruited foreign mercenaries, while Mughals relied on local peasants.
Mughals had a powerful navy on the Atlantic, while Ottomans had no access to water.

questions.text: Which territory, captured under **Suleiman the Magnificent** (1520–1566), gave the Ottoman Empire critical naval dominance in the Eastern Mediterranean and control over numerous trade routes?
correct: Rhodes and much of the North African coast.

questions.text: The Ottoman Sieges of **Vienna** (1529 and 1683) marked a critical turning point. What is the historical *significance* of the failure to capture Vienna in the broader context of land-based empires?
correct: It marked the westernmost limit of the Ottoman Empire's expansion into Europe and signified the beginning of their long-term decline relative to European powers.
incorrect: It immediately led to the total collapse of the Holy Roman Empire.
It caused the Ottomans to adopt Shi'a Islam.
It forced the Ottomans to abandon the use of gunpowder entirely.

questions.text: Define the term **'Armed Trade'** in the context of empire building (1450-1750), and provide one example.
correct: The reliance on heavily armed forces to secure and protect valuable trade routes, often through coercion or outright conquest. Example: The Safavid-Mughal conflict over Kandahar (Silk Road) or the Ottoman control of the Mediterranean.
incorrect: A system where all trade was based on bartering weapons.
A form of commerce where all merchants were required to serve in the military.
A system where only government officials were allowed to trade.

questions.text: The **Qing Dynasty's** expansion into Central Asia (Xinjiang, Tibet) controlled massive land areas, but what was the primary, continuous political challenge this vast territory created for the Qing government?
correct: Managing and maintaining allegiance among diverse ethnic, religious, and nomadic groups (like Mongols and Uyghurs) who resisted centralized Han-style rule.
incorrect: Preventing the entire population of the area from immigrating to the Americas.
The territory immediately became the only source of silver currency.
The required conversion of all subjects to Christianity.

questions.text: The **Safavid Empire** was established by **Shah Ismail I**. What *military* group, characterized by their distinct red headgear, was essential to his early conquests and formed the core of the Safavid army?
What major military technological advantage did **Babur** use in the **Battle of Panipat (1526)**, which allowed his smaller army to defeat the Delhi Sultanate and establish the **Mughal Empire**?
correct: The effective use of gunpowder artillery (cannons) and mobile field tactics.
incorrect: An army composed entirely of salaried European soldiers.
The development of the first military aircraft.

questions.text: Compare the **Ottoman Devshirme** system with the Japanese **Salaried Samurai** system. What *shared purpose* did these two distinct methods serve for their respective central governments?
correct: To create a class of professional military and bureaucratic elites who were loyal only to the central ruler, rather than to local power structures or hereditary nobility.
incorrect: To eliminate all private land ownership within the empire.
To solely convert subject populations to the ruler's religion.
To dismantle existing tax collection systems in favor of tribute lists.

questions.text: The European notion of **Divine Right** was utilized to legitimize rule. What *preceding historical shift* (c. 1300-1600) made it necessary for European monarchs to adopt this highly centralized, religiously-based claim to absolute authority?
correct: The weakening of the Roman Catholic Church's political power and the rise of nation-states, requiring a new justification for absolute secular rule.
incorrect: The widespread adoption of Confucianism in Western Europe.
The collapse of the Atlantic trade network.
The invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg.

questions.text: Analyze the **Mughal Zamindar** system and the **Ottoman Tax Farming** system. Despite differences, what *shared negative consequence* did both innovative tax systems eventually have on the respective empires?
correct: Both systems allowed local agents (Zamindars or tax farmers) to accumulate excessive power and wealth by keeping a surplus of collected revenue, leading to corruption and reduced state revenue over time.
incorrect: Both led to the complete abolition of all military expenditure.
Both forced a massive migration of peasants into urban areas to find work.
Both exclusively relied on collecting taxes in precious metals like silver.

questions.text: The construction of the **Mughal mausolea (like the Taj Mahal)** and **European palaces (like Versailles)** served a similar function in legitimizing power. This falls primarily under which of the **SPICE** (Social, Political, Interaction, Cultural, Economic) characteristics?
correct: Political (demonstrating the ruler's immense wealth, power, and prestige to both domestic and international audiences, consolidating central authority).
incorrect: Economic (since they generated little direct income).
Interaction (since they primarily served an internal political function).
Social (as they did not substantially alter the class structure).

questions.text: The **Qing Imperial Portraits** were a form of **artistic legitimacy**. What was the primary *political message* conveyed by these portraits, especially to non-Manchu subjects?
correct: They presented the emperor as a wise, cultured, and benevolent ruler in the traditional Chinese Confucian style, blending Manchu and Han identity to reinforce the legitimacy of the dynasty.
incorrect: They depicted the emperor as a European Christian saint.
They solely showed the emperor in military gear preparing for war.
They were used to announce the end of all taxation.

questions.text: Explain how the construction and maintenance of the **Palace of Versailles** helped King Louis XIV of France consolidate his **absolute monarchy**.
correct: By requiring the powerful French nobility to reside at Versailles, Louis XIV distracted them with court ritual and prevented them from building regional power bases or challenging his central authority.
incorrect: Versailles was primarily used as a religious pilgrimage site.
Its massive size scared foreign enemies into submission.
It housed the first modern bank of France.

questions.text: The **Mexica (Aztec) tribute lists** primarily collected what *types* of goods and services from subject populations, and what did this reveal about the structure of their empire?
correct: They collected staple goods (maize, beans, cotton), luxury items (feathers, jade), and labor (soldiers/slaves), indicating an empire focused on resource extraction rather than deep administrative integration.
incorrect: They only collected services rendered by highly trained bureaucrats.
They only collected silver and gold coins minted in Europe.
They collected only large quantities of gunpowder and cannons.

questions.text: The **Ming practice of collecting taxes in hard currency (silver)** had a profound **Economic** impact. What *global consequence* resulted from this centralized monetary policy?
correct: It created an immense demand for silver worldwide, driving global trade and funneling much of the New World's silver (from places like Potosi) into China.
incorrect: It caused the Ming currency to completely collapse overnight.
It forced the Ming dynasty to adopt Christianity.
It led to the closure of all major seaports.

questions.text: The **Incan Sun Temple of Cuzco (Qorikancha)** was used to legitimize power. How did this monument connect the Incan rulers to a **Religious** concept of authority?
correct: It was dedicated to Inti (the Sun God) and housed the remains of past emperors, reinforcing the belief that the Sapa Inca (ruler) was a descendant of the Sun God.
incorrect: It was used to house all the gunpowder weapons in the empire.
It served as a central mint for the empire's silver currency.
It was a school for the training of bureaucratic elites.

questions.text: The **Songhai Empire** rulers, such as Askia the Great, actively promoted **Islam**. How did this religious policy help them **consolidate power** over their vast West African territory?
correct: It strengthened trade connections and legitimacy with Muslim merchants and states across the Trans-Saharan network, while providing a unifying legal code (Sharia) and administrative structure.
incorrect: It was a direct order from the King of Spain.
It required the complete elimination of their standing army.
It led to the complete abandonment of the gold-salt trade.

questions.text: The **Ottoman** political system was highly centralized. If the Sultan was the absolute ruler, who held the most administrative and executive authority in the day-to-day governance of the empire, often meeting in the Imperial Council (Divan)?
The **Mexica practice of human sacrifice** served a crucial **Political** function in maintaining imperial control. Explain this function.
correct: It served as a highly visible, terrifying demonstration of the Mexica's power and religious authority, intimidating subject peoples and rivals into compliance.
incorrect: It was a form of tax collection for all citizens.
It was a method for peacefully resolving diplomatic disputes.
It was the primary method for constructing monumental architecture.

questions.text: The **Ottoman Devshirme** system recruited young boys primarily from which specific religious and geographical demographic within the empire?
correct: Christian communities in the Balkans (Southeastern Europe).

Belief_Systems_Continuity_and_Change_Top.name: Belief Systems: Continuity and Change (Topic 3.3) - 12 Qs

questions.text: How did the **Protestant Reformation**'s central theological tenet—the emphasis on individual Bible reading (**Sola Scriptura**) and its promotion of **vernacular printing**—set the stage for the later **Scientific Revolution and Enlightenment**?
correct: It fostered a culture of individual critical inquiry, questioning established authority, and increased literacy across Europe, which were essential precursors to new scientific and philosophical thought.
incorrect: It strictly limited the number of printing presses in Europe to control the spread of new ideas.
It caused the immediate reunification of the Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches.
It led to the adoption of a unified European currency.

questions.text: Explain how the geopolitical rivalry between the **Ottoman (Sunni)** and **Safavid (Shi’a)** empires transformed a religious difference into a *lasting cultural and political division* across the Middle East. **(TEA Format)**
correct: The continuous conflict over territory and trade routes, such as the Ottoman control of Baghdad, turned theological differences into state-sponsored political identities. The Safavids established Shi'a Islam as the state religion of Persia (Iran), permanently differentiating the Iranian cultural sphere from the largely Sunni Ottoman, Mughal, and other Muslim-dominated regions. This institutionalized split became a defining feature of the region's political landscape.
incorrect: The rivalry was solely economic and had no bearing on religious identity after the 17th century.
It led both empires to completely abandon trade with Europe in favor of internal markets.
It resulted in the Safavids converting entirely to Sikhism to find a neutral religious ground.

questions.text: The development of **Sikhism** in South Asia is a powerful example of **syncretism**. What does its emergence *infer* about the cultural and political environment of the **Mughal Empire** in the late 15th to early 17th centuries?
correct: The environment was generally culturally diverse and tolerant enough (especially under early emperors like Akbar) to allow for the peaceful blending of Hindu and Islamic philosophical traditions, although persecution later intensified under Aurangzeb.
incorrect: It infers that the Mughal Empire had completely outlawed both Hinduism and Islam.
It infers that the only major cultural interaction occurring was with European trade companies.
It infers that the Safavid Empire had politically annexed all of South Asia.

questions.text: The **Catholic Reformation (Counter-Reformation)** was the Catholic Church's response to the Protestant challenge. Which *two* policies were central to this movement?
correct: The Council of Trent (reaffirming Catholic doctrine, reforming corrupt practices).
The founding of the Society of Jesus (Jesuits) for education and missionary work.
incorrect: Abolishing the power of the Pope.
Mandating the use of the vernacular language in all services.

questions.text: The **Peace of Westphalia (1648)**, which ended the Thirty Years' War (a religious conflict), is a key **Political** outcome of the Reformation. What enduring principle did it establish in Europe?
correct: The principle of state sovereignty, where the ruler of a state determined its religion (*cuius regio, eius religio*), weakening the power of the Catholic Church and the Holy Roman Empire.
incorrect: It established a single, unified government for all of Europe.
It outlawed all forms of Protestantism.
It mandated that all European trade be managed by the Ottoman Empire.

questions.text: The **Ottoman Empire** managed its diverse non-Muslim populations through the **Millet System**. Explain the *social and cultural* continuity this system allowed.
correct: The Millet System granted non-Muslim religious communities (like Christians and Jews) a degree of autonomous self-governance under their own religious laws and leaders, allowing for cultural continuity in exchange for loyalty and taxes.
incorrect: It required all religious minorities to convert to Shi'a Islam.
It forced all non-Muslims to live in designated, isolated ghettos.
It was a specialized military recruitment system.

questions.text: How did **Shah Abbas I** of the Safavid Empire use religious identity to **consolidate his power** and create a distinct national identity for Persia (Iran)?
correct: He aggressively enforced and promoted Shi'a Islam as the state religion, effectively defining Persian identity in opposition to the neighboring Sunni Ottoman and Mughal Empires.
incorrect: He converted the entire royal court to Catholicism.
He established the *jizya* tax on all Shi'a Muslims.
He eliminated all religious differences within the empire.

questions.text: The **Sunni/Shi’a** split originated in the 7th century C.E. What was the *original* core political disagreement that caused this division?
correct: Disagreement over who should succeed the Prophet Muhammad as the leader (Caliph/Imam) of the Islamic community.
incorrect: Disagreement over the use of gunpowder weapons.
Disagreement over whether or not to trade with China.
Disagreement over the construction of the Taj Mahal.

questions.text: The **Guru Granth Sahib** is the holy scripture of Sikhism. What is the fundamental, syncretic teaching contained within it regarding the relationship between the spiritual beliefs of Hinduism and Islam?
correct: It advocates for a single, non-anthropomorphic God (Islam) while stressing concepts like reincarnation and karma (Hinduism), uniting core tenets of both faiths.
incorrect: It promotes the Divine Right of the Mughal Emperor.
It completely rejects the concept of God in favor of secularism.
It insists on human sacrifice to appease the gods.

questions.text: The blending of elements from two or more distinct cultural or religious traditions to create a new, hybrid tradition is known as **Syncretism**. What is an example of syncretism *outside* of Sikhism in this time period?
correct: Divine Right of Kings (blending religious authority with secular political power) or Sufism (blending Islamic and sometimes Hindu mysticism).
incorrect: The simple conquest of one territory by another.
The exclusive use of gunpowder weaponry.
The construction of a single-purpose fortress.

questions.text: Compare **European Divine Right** with the **Chinese Mandate of Heaven**. What is the key **difference** in how each concept justifies the *removal* of a ruler?
correct: Divine Right argues the king is accountable only to God and cannot be justly overthrown, whereas the Mandate of Heaven argues the ruler *can* be overthrown if they rule unjustly (losing Heaven's mandate).
incorrect: Divine Right allows any peasant to depose the king; Mandate of Heaven does not.
Mandate of Heaven requires the ruler to be Catholic; Divine Right requires them to be Buddhist.
Divine Right only applies to emperors, Mandate of Heaven only to kings.

questions.text: The **Wahhabi Movement** (emerging in the 18th century) demonstrates a later **continuity** in Islamic history. What was the central reformist goal of this movement?
correct: To return Islam to a 'pure' and literal interpretation of the Quran and Hadith, rejecting later practices and Sufi influences seen as deviations.
incorrect: To convert all Muslims to Christianity.
To establish a unified global government under the Ottoman Sultan.
To completely secularize the government and legal code.

Factual_Recall_and_SPICE_Essentials_High.name: Factual Recall and SPICE Essentials (High-Yield Facts) - 13 Qs

questions.text: Identify the **Political** characteristic of the **Qing Dynasty** that allowed them to successfully rule over the Han Chinese population while maintaining Manchu elite status.
correct: The dual administration system, where key bureaucratic positions were shared between Manchu and Han officials.
incorrect: Abolishing the use of the Confucian Civil Service Exams.
Mandating the conversion of all Han Chinese to Manchu shamanism.
Using the Devshirme system to recruit soldiers.

questions.text: What was the **Cultural** change initiated by the Mughal Emperor **Akbar** regarding religious policy, which temporarily eased tensions between Hindus and Muslims in the empire?
correct: Abolishing the *jizya* (tax on non-Muslims) and actively promoting religious tolerance.
incorrect: Establishing the Divine Right of Kings as a legitimizing factor.
Adopting Shi'a Islam as the mandatory state religion.
The construction of the Palace of Versailles.

questions.text: Which **Economic** activity was absolutely critical to the **Ottoman Empire's** financial success, both for expansion and maintenance, from the 15th to 17th centuries?
correct: Controlling key trade choke points, including the Bosporus, Dardanelles, and major land routes connecting Europe and Asia.
incorrect: Exclusive reliance on the silver mining of the Potosi mine.
Generating all state income through the sale of silk textiles.

questions.text: The defining **Cultural** characteristic of the **Safavid Empire** that separated it from its major land-based neighbors (Ottomans and Mughals) was its establishment of what religious branch as the official state faith?
For an LEQ on land-based empires (1450-1750), which event provides the **best contextual link** from the preceding period (c. 1200-1450)?
correct: The **Mongol Empire's** creation of a vast, integrated Eurasian network, which, despite its collapse, left behind military and administrative precedents that successor states like the Ottomans and Mughals adapted.
incorrect: The First Punic War between Rome and Carthage.
The Neolithic Revolution and the start of settled agriculture.
The invention of the steam engine in Britain.

questions.text: Who was the Central Asian conqueror who defeated the Delhi Sultanate in 1526 to establish the **Mughal Empire**?
Which land-based empire utilized **Zamindars** (local officials who collected taxes and maintained a small private army) to administer its revenue system?
In 1453, the **Ottoman Empire** captured the key city of Constantinople. What was the *new name* given to this city, which became the Ottoman capital?
The **Qing Dynasty** was established in 1644 following the collapse of the Ming. What ethnic group from Manchuria led the conquest and ruled the new dynasty?
The **Songhai Empire** (c. 1464–1591) was located in which geographical region, vital for the gold-salt trade?
The **Janissaries** were the elite infantry units of the **Ottoman Empire**. They were recruited and trained through which specific administrative and military system?
Which innovative tax-collection system, involving private citizens bidding for the right to collect taxes in a given district, was used extensively by the **Ottoman Empire**?
What was the single most fundamental **Religious** difference that intensified rivalry between the **Ottoman Empire** and the **Safavid Empire**?
correct: Ottomans were overwhelmingly Sunni, while the Safavids were Shi'a.
incorrect: Ottomans practiced Buddhism, while Safavids practiced Hinduism.
Ottomans believed in Divine Right, while Safavids believed in the Mandate of Heaven.
Ottomans were Christian, while Safavids were Muslim.

questions.text: What years are considered the peak of influence for land-based empires in this unit?
List the three main methods land-based empires relied on to consolidate power during this period.
incorrect: Relying solely on nomadic tribal leaders

questions.text: What invention spread via trade routes and became a powerful source of change for all land-based empires between 1450 and 1750?
What political characteristic defined the 'new monarchies' in Europe in the 1500s?
correct: Centralized control over taxes, the military, and religion
incorrect: Reliance on the Holy Roman Emperor for all decisions

questions.text: Which English king was a strong proponent of the **'Divine Right of Kings'**?
What was the role of the **'Justices of the Peace'** in Tudor England?
correct: To maintain peace and carry out the monarch's laws in the counties
incorrect: To advise the king on military strategy
To collect taxes from foreign merchants

questions.text: Which document signed in **1689** assured individual civil liberties and political power for Parliament in England?
Who was the French monarch known as the **'Sun King'** who built the Palace of Versailles?
What was the primary role of the **'intendants'** in the French government under Louis XIV?
correct: To execute the orders of the central government and collect taxes
incorrect: To lead the army in battle
To serve as religious advisors to the King

questions.text: Which five major land-based empires reached their peak wealth and influence between 1450 and 1750?
Which Russian ruler was crowned tsar in 1547 and expanded the border eastward by seizing land from the descendants of the Golden Horde?
What was the name of the fierce peasant warriors Peter the Great utilized to fight local tribes and expand Russia into Siberia?
By 1639, how far east had the Russian empire advanced, demonstrating their immense territorial gains?
incorrect: Only as far as the Ural Mountains
To the border of the Holy Roman Empire

questions.text: Which Russian ruler is known for westernizing and modernizing the Russian military and government by creating a navy and moving the capital?
What was the **primary reason** Peter the Great moved the Russian capital from Moscow to St. Petersburg?
correct: To gain a warm-water port on the Baltic and keep watch on the Boyars
incorrect: To make the capital closer to Siberia
To align the capital with Confucian principles

questions.text: What was the name of the noble landowning class in Russia that Ivan IV sought to control and diminish?
What paramilitary force did Ivan IV establish to control the boyars, often persecuting them and their families?
What was the fate of most Russian peasants as debts increased throughout this period?
correct: They sank into debt and were tied to the land as serfs
incorrect: They were recruited into the Oprichnina

questions.text: The Winter Palace in St. Petersburg was an example of which architectural style, reflecting Peter the Great's desire to westernize?
What type of tax replaced the land tax in Russia in 1718, designed to increase revenue for the state?
Which dynasty did the Manchu seize power from in 1644 to establish the Qing Dynasty?
What ethnic group from Manchuria seized power in China in 1644, creating the final imperial dynasty?
Which Qing ruler presided over a period of stability and expansion, sending forces into Taiwan and Central Asia, including Tibet?
Which Uighur Muslim region was annexed by Emperor Qianlong after forces failed to defeat the local population, leading to mass killings?
Why did the **White Lotus Rebellion** occur during the later part of Emperor Qianlong’s reign?
correct: High taxes and a desire to restore the Ming Dynasty
incorrect: A desire to adopt Western democracy

questions.text: Which three powerful daimyo are credited with beginning the process of unifying Japan in the late 1500s?
What was the **'Period of Great Peace'** in Japan?
correct: The era of stable rule under the Tokugawa Shogunate
incorrect: The period before the Mongol invasions
The time of the Meiji Restoration
The era of constant civil war between daimyos

questions.text: What form of government did the Tokugawa Shogunate represent?
How did the Tokugawa government control the powerful **daimyos** (aristocrats)?
correct: Required them to maintain residences in both their home territory and the capital (Edo/Tokyo)
incorrect: Forced them all to convert to Christianity
Required them to pay a tax in gunpowder weapons

questions.text: What weapon helped the daimyo unify Japan in the late 1500s?
What three traits did the leaders of the Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal empires share?
Who was the Mongol-Turkic ruler whose invasion and subsequent power vacuum set the stage for the rise of the Gunpowder Empires?
What was the **'ghazi ideal'** that motivated many early Ottoman and other Turkic warriors?
correct: A model for warrior life blending nomadic values with serving as a holy fighter for Islam
incorrect: A code of chivalry based on European knights
Exclusive focus on sea trade and commerce

questions.text: Which city did **Mehmed II** conquer in 1453, and what did he rename it?
What major strait did the city of Istanbul control, making it an economic and military choke point?
Under which sultan did the Ottoman Empire reach its absolute peak, known for his vast conquests and legal code?
What system did the Ottomans use to recruit Christian boys for the military and government bureaucracy?
What was the elite infantry force in the Ottoman army, recruited via the *Devshirme* system, called?
What was **'tax farming'** in the Ottoman Empire, which led to corruption and decreased state revenue?
correct: Local officials and private collectors levied taxes on peasants and skimmed money for themselves
incorrect: A system where all taxes were paid directly to the Sultan
A tax only on farming land, not on merchants
A process where all bureaucrats were paid with farmland

questions.text: What naval battle in **1571** signaled the decline of Ottoman naval dominance in the Mediterranean against European forces?
The Safavid dynasty had its origins in which mystical Islamic order?
Who was the first Shah of the Safavid Empire, proclaimed in 1501?
What specific branch of Islam did the Safavids establish as the unifying state faith, creating a permanent rivalry with their neighbors?
Which major empire was the Safavid Empire's main rival and neighbor to the West?
What was a major cause of conflict between the Ottomans and Safavids besides religious differences?
correct: Control of overland trade routes (e.g., the Silk Roads)
incorrect: Control of the Trans-Saharan slave trade

questions.text: Which Safavid ruler presided over the empire at its height (1588–1629), known for religious tolerance and military strength?
What rights did Safavid women generally retain under Islamic law, even when men had dominant power?
incorrect: The right to serve in the military
The right to rule as Shah

questions.text: Why did the Safavid Empire decline rapidly after Shah Abbas?
correct: Ineffectual leaders, lavish spending, and falling revenues
incorrect: A massive earthquake destroyed the capital
The invasion of the Russian army
Mass conversion of the population to Sunni Islam

questions.text: Which group sacked the Safavid capital of **Isfahan** in 1722, effectively ending Safavid power?
Where did the Safavid rulers often recruit soldiers (similar to the Janissaries) to offset the power of local Turkish tribes?
correct: Christian boys pressed into service (e.g., from Georgia in Russia)

questions.text: Who founded the Mughal dynasty in the 1520s after defeating the Delhi Sultanate with gunpowder weapons?
Which Mughal ruler is known for his religious tolerance, unifying taxes, and creating a strong centralized government from 1556 to 1605?
What were **'zamindars'** in the Mughal Empire?
correct: Paid government officials in charge of duties like taxation, construction, and water supply
incorrect: Elite infantry units of the military
Chief merchants of the Silk Road trade
Religious judges in the court system

questions.text: What is the name of the magnificent mausoleum built by Shah Jahan in honor of his wife?
Which Mughal ruler reversed Akbar's policies, tried to rid the empire of Hindu influences, and drained the treasury on fruitless wars?
What was the **'Din-i Ilahi'** ('Divine Faith')?
correct: A religion promoted by Akbar to reconcile Hinduism and Islam
incorrect: The name of the Mughal royal court

questions.text: What ritual involving widows did Akbar try to prohibit, though with limited success?
correct: Sati (widows killing themselves on their husbands' funeral pyres)

questions.text: What religion developed in South Asia from the interaction and syncretism of Hinduism and Islam?
Which groups rebelled against the Mughals due to the religious intolerance of later rulers like Aurangzeb?
Which European powers gained economic power in India as the Mughals declined?
How did rulers in land-based empires typically finance their bureaucracies and standing militaries?
incorrect: Printing large amounts of paper money

questions.text: What was the **'divine right of kings'**?
correct: The claim that the right to rule was given to a king by God
incorrect: The right to choose the state religion
The duty to rule justly and kindly

questions.text: How did the **Palace of Versailles** help King Louis XIV consolidate absolute power?
correct: It kept nobles close to him, making it difficult for them to plot against him or build regional power
incorrect: It was the sole source of military recruitment
It housed the entire French treasury
It was an early site of industrial factories

questions.text: What ancient system did the Ming Dynasty revive to improve education and the quality of the bureaucracy?
How did the Aztec (Mexica) Empire primarily control its territory, relying on resource extraction rather than deep administration?
correct: It relied on a decentralized tributary empire that extracted wealth and labor from conquered states
incorrect: It used a centralized bureaucracy based on a written constitution
It used a system of salaried officials paid in silver currency
It granted local lords complete autonomy

questions.text: What was the common name for the **Devshirme** system in the Ottoman Empire, reflecting its nature?
How were taxes initially collected in the Ming Dynasty, before later requiring payment in silver currency?
correct: In the form of grains and other goods

questions.text: The **'tribute system'** (used by China and the Aztecs) required conquered states to provide what to the central power?
Why did the Gunpowder Empires (Ottoman, Safavid, Mughal) fail to modernize their military and economies compared to Russia or Europe?
correct: They did not modernize their armies, administration, or industrial capacity as quickly as European powers
incorrect: They were constantly ravaged by massive plagues
They lacked access to sea trade

questions.text: What role did **'harem politics'** play in the decline of the Ottoman Empire?
correct: Wives and concubines promoted their own children, leading to inexperienced and weak sultans
