correct: Military professional (used to maintain centralized control)

questions.text: The Tokugawa Shogunate limited foreign influence and trade, a policy that is an example of what larger trend shared with the Qing Dynasty?
What was the significance of the Shogunate's 1588 edict forbidding farmers to possess weapons (the 'sword hunt')?
correct: It eliminated the chance of armed peasant uprisings and reinforced the social hierarchy
incorrect: It started a civil war with the daimyos
It made the Samurai class obsolete
It led to the immediate downfall of the Shogunate

questions.text: The Tokugawa Shogunate's establishment of the capital at **Edo (Tokyo)** was a move primarily intended to benefit which group?
What major infrastructure project was essential for transporting grains to military locations during the Ming Dynasty?
During the Ming Dynasty, after 1580, which factors led to the dynasty's eventual bankruptcy? (Select all that apply)
incorrect: The complete collapse of the silver market

questions.text: How did the Manchu (Qing) Dynasty effectively use the existing Chinese Civil Service Examination system?
correct: To ensure their authority by integrating the Chinese scholar-gentry into the administration
incorrect: To promote only Manchu people to government positions
To convert civil servants to Christianity

questions.text: Emperor Qianlong's campaigns against **Burma and Vietnam** in the later part of his reign were significant because they resulted in what?
correct: Unsuccessful and costly wars that emptied the empire's treasury
incorrect: The annexation of all of Southeast Asia
The discovery of massive gold reserves

Part_III_Islamic_Empires__Consolidation_.name: Part III: Islamic Empires - Consolidation, Conflict, and Decline (40 Q)

questions.text: The Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal Empires all shared a common descent from what cultural/ethnic group?
incorrect: Arab merchants from the Arabian Peninsula

questions.text: The **ghazi ideal**, which served as a model for the Gunpowder Empires' warriors, blended nomadic culture with what specific religious goal?
correct: Willingness to serve as a holy fighter for Islam

questions.text: The invasions of **Tamerlane** were significant because he failed to leave behind what crucial element in the areas he conquered, leading to economic issues?
Under **Mehmed II**, the Ottoman Empire captured Constantinople in 1453, largely due to the use of what military technology?
The city of **Istanbul** (formerly Constantinople) prospered under the Ottomans because it controlled which vital trade chokepoint?
The Ottoman practice of **devshirme** was a system for recruiting which two groups into loyal service?
The **Janissaries** were unique in the Ottoman Empire because they were soldiers and bureaucrats who were fiercely loyal only to whom?
Under **Suleiman I**, the Ottoman navy captured the island of **Rhodes** in 1522, which had long been a stronghold of which group?
What was the long-term impact of the Ottomans failing to capture **Vienna** (Austria) in 1529 and again in 1683?
correct: It marked a turning point in Ottoman domination in Eastern Europe
incorrect: It led to the immediate end of the Ottoman Empire
It caused the Safavids to conquer the Ottoman territory
It led to the conversion of the Sultan to Christianity

questions.text: The term **'harem politics'** refers to the post-Suleiman period in the Ottoman Empire where power behind the scenes was often exerted by whom?
correct: Wives and concubines of the Sultan promoting their children as heirs
incorrect: Elected members of the national assembly
The heads of the Janissary corps

questions.text: The **Battle of Lepanto (1571)** resulted in an Ottoman naval defeat against a force led primarily by which European power?
The **Millet System** in the Ottoman Empire allowed which minority religious groups to run their own affairs under the overall authority of the Sultan?
The Safavid Empire was unique among the Gunpowder Empires for its strict adherence to which branch of Islam?
The Safavid military under **Shah Abbas I** was modernized by importing weaponry and relying on which group for military advice?
The **Qizilbash** (Red Heads) were the initial core of the Safavid military, consisting of what type of militants who helped establish the empire?
The core conflict between the **Ottoman (Sunni)** and **Safavid (Shi'a)** Empires, besides religious disagreement, centered on control over what?
The Ottomans used **trade embargoes** (official bans on trade) against the Safavid **silk traders** as a way to assert what over their eastern rival?
Despite being restricted in movement and veiled, Safavid women were permitted access to which specific rights under Islamic law?
incorrect: Serving in the highest political offices

questions.text: The decline of the Safavid Empire began after the leaders who followed Shah Abbas I combined lavish lifestyles and military spending with what economic problem?
incorrect: A collapse of the silk industry
The complete abolition of all taxes

questions.text: In 1722, the Safavids were overthrown when they failed to quell a rebellion by the heavily oppressed Sunni Pashtuns in which modern-day region?
The **Mughal Empire** was founded by **Babur**, who was a descendant of which Mongol-Turkic ruler?
During Akbar's relatively peaceful reign, overseas trade flourished with which group conducting most of the commerce?
The Mughal Empire's overseas trade involved exporting textiles, spices, and precious stones, often in exchange for what imports?
Internal trade within the Mughal Empire was carried on by which unique hereditary social groups?
Akbar the Great attempted to create a syncretic approach to religion by using which blend of Hindu and Islamic traditions to unite his subjects?
Akbar's policy of religious tolerance led to the abolition of what specific tax on non-Muslims?
The **Taj Mahal** serves as an example of Mughal monumental architecture, constructed under Shah Jahan to memorialize whom?
Aurangzeb, Shah Jahan’s successor, inherited a Mughal Empire already weakened by what two factors?
correct: Failure to keep up with military innovations
incorrect: The abolition of the caste system

questions.text: Aurangzeb's policies are characterized by an intolerance of minority religions, sparking uprisings among which two groups?
Aurangzeb's expansion of the empire to the south led to the draining of the empire’s treasury and failed to suppress which type of internal conflict?
incorrect: The rise of a powerful navy

questions.text: The Songhai Empire was primarily focused on consolidating power and wealth from which vital trade route?
The wealth of the Songhai Empire under Askia the Great was primarily derived from trading which two commodities?
Askia the Great consolidated Songhai's power by creating what type of organized structure to oversee tributary states in the Niger Valley?
correct: A centralized bureaucracy of governors and officials

questions.text: The Songhai Empire was brought to an end in 1591 after its capital of Gao was sacked by forces from which neighboring Muslim power?
The Mughal and Safavid Empires conflicted over control of resources and trade routes in which present-day region?
Which of the Gunpowder Empires was able to survive as an independent nation-state into the modern era because it successfully modernized its army based on European models?
The Ottoman Empire eventually became known by which derogatory nickname due to its long decline and loss of territory?
In the later period of decline, the Ottoman Empire suffered from the expansionist tendencies of which powerful European rival?
The Safavid leaders who followed Shah Abbas were described as having lavish lifestyles and military spending combined with what other factor, leading to collapse?

Aurangzeb's insistence on a pious Islamic lifestyle and intolerance of minority religions led to the destabilization of the Mughal Empire and allowed which two European powers to gain economic power in India?

Part_IV_CrossImperial_Administration_and.name: Part IV: Cross-Imperial Administration and Legitimacy (20 Q)

questions.text: The **Zamindar system (Mughal)** and **Tax Farming (Ottoman)** were similar in that they both eventually led to what negative administrative consequence?
correct: Increased corruption and the development of semi-autonomous local power brokers
incorrect: The rise of a national banking system
Eliminating the need for a standing army

questions.text: The **Devshirme system (Ottoman)** and the **Civil Service Exam (Qing)** were similar in that both created a bureaucratic elite whose primary loyalty was to whom?

In the Ottoman Empire, **Tax Farmers** paid a fixed annual sum to the central government and then recouped the money by collecting taxes from residents. This system often resulted in what for the tax farmers?
correct: Collecting significantly more money than they paid out, enriching themselves
incorrect: Always losing money and going bankrupt
Being required to join the Janissary corps
Being elected to the highest offices

questions.text: The **Aztec Empire** was considered a **tributary empire** because its main source of revenue came from what, rather than direct centralized tax collection?
correct: Yearly offerings and tributes from surrounding conquered areas

questions.text: The Aztec Empire maintained control over its territory primarily through what three methods, rather than a centralized bureaucracy?
Which religious title was used by rulers in Islamic empires to legitimize their rule as successors to the Prophet Muhammad?
The use of **monumental architecture** by rulers like Shah Jahan (Taj Mahal) and Louis XIV (Versailles) was used to legitimize their rule by doing what?
incorrect: Demonstrating commitment to simplicity and austerity
Building public housing for the poor

questions.text: Which specific monumental achievement did the Mughal Empire use to distinguish its architectural legitimacy by blending Islamic and Hindu/Indian traditions?
The **Songhai Empire** employed bureaucrats from which specialized class to run its administration?
correct: The scholarly class educated in Islamic schools

questions.text: The Portuguese and other Europeans primarily arrived in East Asia during the Ming Dynasty aiming to do what?
correct: Encroach on the Asian trade network
incorrect: Provide military aid to the Manchu

questions.text: The **Caste System (jatis)** in India, although a Hindu social grouping, continued to influence Mughal society by being the basis for what two opportunities?
Which region was subject to the Ottoman practice of **Tax Farming** that was also used to pay the salaries of some Janissaries?
The Safavid military recruited soldiers from as far away as **Georgia in Russia**, often pressuring which group into service?
The use of **Boyars** (Russian nobility) in Peter the Great's government was undermined by his introduction of what system to create new officials?
The principle of **religious tolerance** practiced by Akbar in the Mughal Empire was fundamentally *different* from the religious tolerance of the Safavids in what way?
correct: Akbar promoted cooperation with non-Muslims, while the Safavids only promoted one sect of Islam (Shi'a)
incorrect: Akbar founded a Catholic mission, while the Safavids did not
Akbar forced all his subjects to convert to Hinduism, while the Safavids did not
Akbar built only mosques, while the Safavids built only churches

questions.text: What was the core reason that the **Safavid Empire** failed to develop a significant navy despite being on the Arabian Sea?
correct: Its primary focus and military strength were land-based
incorrect: Its entire treasury was spent on the Taj Mahal
It was forbidden by Shi'a Islamic law
It was successfully blockaded by the Qing Dynasty

questions.text: The **Aztec Empire** was less centralized and bureaucratic than the other land empires because it did not have what over its territory within Mesoamerica?
correct: Little to no direct political control

questions.text: The increasing importance of **ocean routes for trade** was a key factor that undermined the power and influence of land-based empires *after* the 18th century.
What form of **taxation** made up the bulk of the taxes collected during the Ming Dynasty, and was initially collected in grain and later in silver?

The **Ottoman Empire** was the only one of the Islamic Gunpowder Empires to have a dual authority that controlled the empire alongside the central government. Which group represented this dual authority?

I_Final_Synthesis_Comparison_and_Imperia.name: I. Final Synthesis, Comparison, and Imperial Decline (25 Q)

questions.text: All major land-based empires during this period (Ottoman, Mughal, Qing) failed to maintain pace with Europe primarily due to their neglect of what two areas?
The **Qizilbash** (Safavid) and the **Boyars** (Russian) were similar in that both represented what class of people whose power the central ruler sought to limit?
In the Ottoman Empire, the long-term impact of **Tax Farming (Iltizam)** was the growth of a wealthy and powerful local elite class known as what?
correct: Multi-generational corruption that contributed to the weakening of the central authority
incorrect: The rise of a republican government

questions.text: The **Aztec tributary system** differed from the centralized tax collection of the Qing Dynasty in that the Aztecs primarily relied on what to secure revenue?
correct: Intimidation and fear rather than deep administrative control
incorrect: A unified currency based on silver

questions.text: What was the economic consequence of the **Mughal Zamindars**' growing power and corruption in the 17th century?
correct: Peasant exploitation and an inability for the central government to collect accurate revenue
incorrect: The sudden end of the textile trade
The rise of a national currency
Massive inflation caused by an abundance of tea

questions.text: Peter the Great's use of **The Table of Ranks** served what purpose in undermining the power of the old Russian nobility?
correct: It allowed non-noble individuals to rise in government and military based on merit and service
incorrect: It forced all nobles to move to Siberia
It required all citizens to serve in the navy

questions.text: In **Tokugawa Japan**, the **'salaried samurai'** system was a transition away from feudalism by making the warrior class dependent on whom for their income?
The Mughal Empire eventually fragmented into regional states, allowing which European power to gain political and economic control?
The **Battle of Lepanto (1571)** and the failure to take **Vienna (1683)** were similar in that both marked what change for the Ottoman Empire?
correct: The slowing or end of major territorial expansion into Europe and the Mediterranean
incorrect: The beginning of the Industrial Revolution
The total collapse of the empire
The conversion of the Sultan to Christianity

questions.text: The Safavid Empire was brought down by internal Sunni Pashtun rebellions after its later leaders engaged in what policy?
correct: Excessive military spending and religious intolerance against Sunnis
incorrect: A massive expansion of religious tolerance
The successful conquest of all of China
The complete abolition of the silk trade

questions.text: Which cultural movement in Europe was fundamentally and intellectually opposed to the idea of the Divine Right of Kings?
The political philosophy of **John Locke** directly challenged the justification for absolute monarchy by asserting what rights?
correct: Natural rights to life, liberty, and property
incorrect: The right to an absolute ruler
The right to a state religion
The right to wage aggressive war

questions.text: What major infrastructure project was essential for transporting grains to military garrisons during the Ming Dynasty?
The **Qing Dynasty's** annexation of new territories like Xinjiang and Tibet was secured through which strategy?
correct: Mass killings, sophisticated use of artillery, and brutal military force
incorrect: Peaceful treaties and trade negotiation only
Granting total independence to all conquered groups

questions.text: Under **Askia the Great**, Songhai's administrative structure was strengthened through the creation of a centralized bureaucracy and a system of what?
The **Jesuits** (Society of Jesus) of the Counter-Reformation excelled in what two areas to spread Catholic influence globally?
What was the eventual fate of the vast majority of peasants in Russia as the state consolidated control and increased debt?
correct: They were reduced to **serfdom**, legally bound to the land
incorrect: They all emigrated to the New World

questions.text: What was the significance of the **Ming Dynasty's** move to collect taxes primarily in silver?
correct: It connected China more deeply into the global economy, reliant on silver from the Americas
incorrect: It completely isolated China from all global trade
It caused massive deflation of currency
It ended the use of the Civil Service Exam

questions.text: Which dynasty in Russia was responsible for both Westernization efforts and the dramatic expansion of the empire into Siberia?
The **Millet System** was most successful at achieving what goal for the Ottoman state?
correct: Maintaining peace and stability among diverse religious populations
incorrect: Forcing all subjects to convert to Islam
Eliminating the need for a tax system
Expanding the empire into new European territories

questions.text: The **Peace of Westphalia** created a new political order in Europe based on which two principles of international relations?
The **Mughal Empire's** early success was largely attributed to the effective use of which military technology?
What was the main outcome of **King Louis XIV**'s revocation of the **Edict of Nantes** in 1685?
correct: The emigration of highly skilled Protestant (Huguenot) artisans and professionals
incorrect: The declaration of religious freedom in France

questions.text: In the Ottoman Empire, the **tax farmers** often engaged in excessive and illegal tax collection, leading to widespread what?
The **Tokugawa Shogunate's** policy of national isolation (*sakoku*) was primarily implemented to prevent what foreign influence?
correct: The spread of Christianity and European political interference

II_ReligiousSocial_Nuances_and_Cultural_.name: II. Religious/Social Nuances and Cultural Legitimacy (25 Q)

questions.text: What was the specific theological innovation introduced by **Martin Luther** regarding salvation?
The primary goal of the **Puritans** in England and North America was to do what to the Anglican Church?
correct: Purify it of all remaining Catholic practices
incorrect: Abolish it and establish a democracy

questions.text: The **Council of Trent** (Counter-Reformation) made two specific structural decisions regarding Catholic practices. Which were they? (Select two)
incorrect: Abolished the authority of the Pope

questions.text: The **Thirty Years' War** (1618–1648) devastatingly reduced the population of which European region by up to 30% in some areas?
In the Mughal Empire, what was the term for the system of land revenue collection often characterized by regional power and corruption?
The **Din-i Ilahi** was Akbar's attempt at syncretism, which borrowed elements from which two primary religions?
The **Taj Mahal** represents the peak of Mughal architecture, primarily combining elements of Persian, Islamic, and which other tradition?
In the Ottoman Empire, the **Grand Bazaar** and other marketplaces served what purpose in terms of legitimizing the Sultan's rule?
correct: Showcasing the wealth and cosmopolitan nature of the empire
incorrect: Serving as the primary military recruiting center
A secret location for tax evasion

questions.text: Which new empire used the **ghazi ideal** (holy warrior for Islam) to inspire its successful military expansion?
The Safavid Empire's decision to aggressively promote **Shi'a Islam** created conflict with the Ottoman Empire and which other neighboring Islamic state?
The **Qing Dynasty's** adoption of the **Civil Service Exam** and the retention of Han administrative structures demonstrated what legitimizing strategy?
correct: Co-opting the existing Chinese elite to maintain stability
incorrect: An attempt to impose Manchu culture exclusively
A move toward a democratic system

questions.text: The Manchu practice of the **'Queue'** hairstyle for Han men was a clear symbol of what political reality?
correct: Manchu dominance and Han Chinese submission
incorrect: A fashion trend borrowed from Europe
A sign of high military rank

questions.text: Peter the Great's forced beard-shaving and mandated European dress for Russian nobles were examples of what broader policy?
Akbar's attempt to ban **Sati** (the ritual burning of Hindu widows) was part of his overall effort to improve the status of which group in society?
The **Inquisition** in Europe served which primary function during the Counter-Reformation?
correct: To punish non-believers, heretics, and enforce Catholic orthodoxy
incorrect: To translate the Bible into vernacular languages
To establish a new form of government

questions.text: The **Songhai Empire** utilized Islam as a unifying force by making it the official religion and promoting what educational feature?
correct: The establishment of Islamic schools and centers of learning
incorrect: The forced conversion of all West Africans
The abolition of all taxes on Muslims
The complete isolation from North Africa

questions.text: What was the Ottoman practice of **Tax Farming** (Iltizam)?
correct: The right to collect taxes was sold to the highest bidder
incorrect: Taxes were collected only in gold and silver
Taxes were determined by the Janissaries

questions.text: The Mughal Emperor **Aurangzeb** is known for reversing Akbar's policies of tolerance by reintroducing which tax?
The construction of the **Palace of Versailles** required enormous financial resources, which were acquired through which economic policy favored by France?
In France, **intendants** were royal officials dispatched to the provinces who performed what key function?
correct: Carried out the orders of the central government, especially regarding tax collection and justice
incorrect: Served as elected representatives for the common people
Led the French navy in battle

questions.text: The Ottoman Empire controlled which major strait, giving it immense strategic and economic power?
In Russia, Peter the Great established the **Holy Synod**, which replaced the Patriarch and was overseen by whom?
correct: A secular official loyal to the Tsar

questions.text: The **Oprichnina**, Ivan IV's personal loyalty force, was responsible for confiscating the lands of which group, thus reducing their political power?
In the period 1450-1750, all major land-based empires were defined by what primary measure of power?
incorrect: The amount of silver in their treasury
The size of their merchant fleet

questions.text: The **Scientific Revolution** fundamentally challenged medieval philosophy by emphasizing what three principles?

III_Granular_Details_and_Forgotten_Facts.name: III. Granular Details and Forgotten Facts (50 Q)

questions.text: The **Tudor Dynasty** in England used the Justices of the Peace to consolidate power under which two monarchs?
The English Civil War (1642–1651) was fought over which core issue regarding power?
correct: The power division between the Monarchy and Parliament
incorrect: The succession of the Ottoman Sultan

questions.text: The **Glorious Revolution (1688)** was unique because it involved almost no bloodshed and established what type of government in England?
What was the significance of the **Edict of Nantes** (1598) in France?
correct: It granted religious toleration to French Protestants (Huguenots)
incorrect: It started the Thirty Years' War
It declared France a Protestant nation
It established the Divine Right of Kings

questions.text: The construction of the Palace of Versailles was funded by the *taille*, which was what type of tax?
correct: A direct land tax levied on the French peasantry
incorrect: A voluntary donation by the nobility

questions.text: In Russia, which family was given the task of leading the early expansion into Siberia by hiring the Cossacks?
The **Charter of the Nobility** (1785) in Russia, issued by Catherine the Great, served to maintain the loyalty of the nobility by doing what?
correct: Granting them special rights and privileges
incorrect: Forcing them to adopt Western culture
Requiring them to pay higher taxes

questions.text: Which territory was a key focus of Russian expansion under Catherine the Great, securing access to the Black Sea?
Under the Qing Dynasty, **Emperor Kangxi** developed a deep relationship with which specific group of Catholic missionaries?
Qianlong's campaign against the **Miao people** in southern China was an example of a policy designed to achieve what?
correct: Expand territory and incorporate new groups, often by force

questions.text: The **White Lotus Rebellion** was one of the first major signs of what problem developing within the late Qing Dynasty?
In **Tokugawa Japan**, the centralized government's control over the daimyos was secured by requiring them to leave which family members permanently in the capital (Edo)?
The **ghazi ideal** was a cultural factor that helped motivate the early expansion of which empire?
Which famous Ottoman Sultan was known as 'the Lawgiver' due to his overhaul of the empire's legal system?
In the Ottoman Empire, the Sultan's chief military and administrative advisor was known by what title?
The **safavid** capital of **Isfahan** became a center of which two specific art forms under Shah Abbas?
The Safavid Empire was particularly dependent on what two overland trade commodities for its wealth?
The **Mughal Empire's** early wealth was derived not just from trade, but also from which primary source of revenue?
correct: A highly effective and centralized land tax system

questions.text: The **jatis** (sub-castes) in the Mughal Empire dictated what two aspects of life, even though the ruling class was Muslim?
The **Portuguese** were the first Europeans to establish a strong maritime presence in India, leading to what eventual Mughal decision?
correct: The granting of trading concessions to other Europeans (British, French, Dutch)
incorrect: An all-out war with the Portuguese
The conversion of the Emperor to Catholicism

questions.text: The **Songhai Empire's** prosperity and centralized power were highly dependent on which major geographical feature?
incorrect: The Sahara Desert (its trade routes, not the desert itself)

questions.text: The **Aztec Empire** used a specific religious ritual involving the massive loss of life of conquered people as a form of what kind of control?
The **Cossacks** were crucial to Russian expansion, but their periodic rebellions against the Russian state were often sparked by what issue?
correct: Lack of support or political control by the central government

questions.text: The **Divine Right of Kings** doctrine was initially formulated by which monarch?
Which military unit in the Ottoman Empire, initially formed through the **Devshirme system**, developed enough power to challenge the Sultan in the later period?
The **Sikh faith**, emerging in the Mughal Empire, was a syncretic religion that borrowed from which two traditions?
The early success of the **Ottoman Empire** was heavily dependent on the military and administrative loyalty of what two specific groups?
Which Russian Tsar was known for brutal repression of the Boyars and establishing the Oprichnina, contributing to a period of instability known as the Time of Troubles?
The **Peace of Augsburg (1555)** was an early attempt to resolve religious conflict in the Holy Roman Empire by establishing what principle?
correct: *Cuius regio, eius religio* (whose realm, his religion)
incorrect: The establishment of a European Parliament
The abolition of the Catholic Church

questions.text: The **Tokugawa Shogunate** in Japan solidified its rule after a period of civil war known as what?
The Mughal Empire's prosperity relied on a steady influx of which foreign currency for its massive domestic and international trade?
What was the significance of the **Ming Dynasty's** reliance on eunuchs within the central government?
correct: Eunuchs were considered less likely to establish hereditary power bases, increasing their loyalty to the Emperor
incorrect: Eunuchs were the only ones permitted to collect taxes
Eunuchs were the only ones allowed to trade with Europeans

questions.text: In the Ottoman Empire, the Sultan's power was often checked by the influence of which group of religious scholars?
The Safavid military under Shah Abbas included a specialized group of Christian slave soldiers known as what?
Which European economic theory of the 17th century encouraged states to maximize exports and minimize imports to accumulate specie (gold/silver)?
The **Mughal** and **Qing** empires both faced which common internal challenge that contributed to their eventual decline?
correct: Massive size and difficulty in maintaining control and communication over vast territories
incorrect: Successful naval invasions by European powers

questions.text: What was the primary economic consequence of the **Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb's** costly military campaigns in Southern India?
correct: They drained the imperial treasury, weakening the central government
incorrect: They resulted in the discovery of new silver mines
They opened new trade routes to China

questions.text: The Songhai Empire's use of a powerful army, including **cavalry**, was vital for controlling which aspect of its economy?
What was the name of the region that served as the primary conflict zone between the Ottoman and Safavid Empires?
The **Peace of Westphalia** allowed the Dutch Republic and which other European political entity to gain formal independence?
Which Russian ruler was instrumental in seizing land from Sweden, securing a critical access point to the Baltic Sea for St. Petersburg?
The Ottoman system of **Tax Farming** was one factor that contributed to the weakening of which older land-grant system?
The **Qing Dynasty** tripled the land area of China by 1911 through successful expansion into which areas?
What specific agricultural product, introduced from the Americas, led to a rapid increase in the Chinese population during the Qing Dynasty?
The decline of the **Tokugawa Shogunate** in Japan was partially caused by an economic policy that favored what group over the merchant class?
Which event in 16th century England allowed the monarchy to seize land and wealth from the church to fund its military and state consolidation?
correct: The English Reformation and the Dissolution of the Monasteries

questions.text: The **Ottoman Empire** was unique among the Gunpowder Empires for its extensive control over what strategic geographical feature?
correct: A large coastline and access to multiple seas (Mediterranean, Black, Red)
incorrect: The vast, frozen tundra of Siberia

questions.text: The **Mughal Empire's** primary weakness, exploited by European powers, was its failure to develop what type of military force?
The **Caste System** in India continued to dictate social standing and occupation under the Mughal Empire, illustrating which concept?
correct: Continuity of deep-seated social hierarchies despite a change in the ruling elite
incorrect: The complete abolition of all social classes
The adoption of European social practices

questions.text: Both the **Russian** and **Qing** empires expanded significantly into vast, sparsely populated lands, which resulted in both empires having to contend with what type of diverse population?
correct: Minority ethnic and religious groups on their borders

questions.text: The Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal empires are referred to as 'Gunpowder Empires' because they...
correct: successfully used cannons and firearms to conquer territory and maintain centralized control
incorrect: were the first civilizations to invent gunpowder in world history
relied exclusively on gunpowder trade for their economic prosperity
were all eventually conquered by European powers with superior gunpowder technology

questions.text: How did rulers like Louis XIV of France and Qing emperors use art and architecture to legitimize power?
correct: By sponsoring magnificent palaces and elaborate art to display their immense wealth and divine authority
incorrect: By banning all forms of artistic expression to prevent potential dissent and rebellion
By promoting minimalist art styles to demonstrate their humility and connection to common people
Art and architecture had no connection to political power during this historical period

questions.text: The Safavid Empire, a 'Gunpowder Empire', was unique among its neighbors (the Ottomans and Mughals) because it officially established which branch of Islam as its state religion?
In Mughal India, Emperor Akbar was known for his policy of religious tolerance, which he demonstrated by...
correct: Abolishing the 'jizya' (tax on non-Muslims) and sponsoring debates between different faiths.
incorrect: Forcibly converting the entire Hindu population to Islam.
Banning all religions except for a new state religion he invented.
Expelling all non-Muslims from the Mughal Empire.

questions.text: The Ming and Qing dynasties of China used which system to recruit government officials, ensuring a bureaucracy based on Confucian principles?
What was the Devshirme system in the Ottoman Empire?
correct: A levy that seized Christian boys from the Balkans to serve in military and administrative roles
incorrect: A voluntary military recruitment program for Muslim families
A tax requiring citizens to provide livestock to the Sultan
A system granting land to soldiers in exchange for service

questions.text: Which regions were primarily targeted for Devshirme collection?
incorrect: Arab provinces such as Syria and Egypt

questions.text: Boys taken through the Devshirme system typically received training for which elite institution?
Which skills were Devshirme recruits trained in? (Select all that apply)
correct: Loyalty to the Sultan above all else

questions.text: Why did the Janissaries become one of the most feared infantry forces in the world?
correct: They were highly disciplined, professionally trained, and equipped with early firearms
incorrect: They used horses and cavalry tactics to defeat opponents
They relied mostly on mercenary soldiers
They fought only in defensive battles within Ottoman territory

questions.text: Which statement best explains why Devshirme boys were loyal to the Sultan?
correct: They were removed from their families, converted to Islam, and raised to depend on the Ottoman state
incorrect: They received large personal land grants and wealth from their home villages
They maintained strong ties with their birth families and Christian communities
They were free to leave Janissary service at any time

questions.text: Which of the following positions could Devshirme recruits rise to? (Select all that apply)
What was a major long-term weakness that developed within the Janissary corps?
correct: They became resistant to reform and increasingly corrupt, weakening military efficiency
incorrect: They abandoned firearms and returned to medieval weapons
They refused to recruit new members after the 1400s

questions.text: In the period 1450–1750, the global balance of power shifted from **land-based empires** (Unit 3) to which new political entity?
The transcript emphasizes that European states did not invent the key maritime technologies, but rather did what with them?

Europeans were exposed to the new Maritime technologies primarily through which context?
correct: Merchant activity along the major trade routes (connected to the Pax Mongolica)
incorrect: Direct naval conquest of Asian ports

questions.text: The **Portuguese Caravel** was intentionally built smaller than ships like the Chinese junk, making it ideal for which two specific characteristics?

incorrect: Carrying metric buttloads of cargo (like the Chinese junk)

correct: Innovative tools that cut the cost of production basically in half
incorrect: The use of inferior wood that was easier to acquire
Exclusive focus on shallow coastal trade routes
Exemptions from all shipbuilding taxes and tariffs

The Aztec capital Tenochtitlan is located on the site of which modern-day metropolis?
The narrator uses the film The Dirty Dozen and the film Armageddon to illustrate the modern concept of what?
In some ways, the Aztec and Inca Empires were 'perfect for Spanish conquest' due to the similar link between what two types of power?
The Spanish decision to adapt the mit'a system suggests a continuity of which pre-conquest labor system in the Americas?
The Spanish considered purchasing African slaves for South American mines inefficient for which reason, besides high altitude issues?
incorrect: African slaves were too expensive for the region
The Pope had banned African slavery in Peru

questions.text: The silver mountain that funded the Spanish Empire and the global economy was located in which modern-day region, implied by the context of Inca territory?
The new wealth from American silver made Spain much more inclined to build what, even though they were later sunk in 1588?
Charles V eventually gave the Austrian half of his kingdom to his son who was named what?
Philip II inherited control over Spain's American holdings, European territory, and which other Pacific region named after him?
The Dutch critique of the Spanish rule that fueled their rebellion was based on religion and what other factor?
The success of England against the Spanish Armada was largely credited to whom?
The historian Dennis Flynn emphasized that China surrendered a significant hunk of its GDP to secure what commodity?
correct: White metal produced mostly in Spanish America and Japan
incorrect: Rare medicinal herbs from the Middle East

questions.text: The Ming government often required Chinese people to produce which specific commodity for the global market?
What was the initial economic state of China that led them to adopt silver coins after their first inflation crisis?
correct: Their economy was so big they ran out of copper or bronze
incorrect: They had no access to any other metal
They were forced to by the Spanish
The Catholic Church mandated silver use

questions.text: The global outsourcing problem, noted in the text, affected silk producers in Spain due to what action by the Chinese government?
correct: Forcing farmers to produce silver-earning goods like silk
incorrect: Implementing high tariffs on European goods
Banning silk production outside of China

questions.text: The Ming government's economic struggles, including failure to peg taxes to inflation, were compounded by overspending on which sector?
The new Ming tax policy requiring silver meant that farmers had to find ways to sell which product to meet their tax burden?
The Incan mit'a system required male peasants to perform unpaid work for the government for what length of time each year?
The Spanish system of administration was easily established because the Aztecs and Inca already had what in place?
The discovery of the silver mountain provided the Spanish crown with an enormous what, to be used for military and political aims?
The decline of the Ming Dynasty in the 17th century was primarily due to economic issues like inflation and excessive spending on which two factors?
correct: Defense and a failure to adjust tax rates
incorrect: Foreign invasion by the Dutch and Portuguese
High wages for bureaucrats and soldiers

questions.text: In the Viceroyalty of New Spain, what type of agricultural operation was commonly run by Spanish aristocrats?
The first truly global market, centered on Spanish American silver, involved Afro-Eurasia through Europe, China, and which other major Asian nation, according to the final line?

Topic_44_Maritime_Empires_Established_an.name: Topic 4.4: Maritime Empires Established and Maintained

questions.text: The primary motivations for European states to develop Maritime Empires are summarized by which three concepts?
The desire to 'enrich themselves' falls under which of the three main motivations for European Maritime Empire building?
The motivation of 'Glory' essentially refers to the European states' desire for what status?
correct: To be the greatest state in the world

questions.text: Which European state was the first to establish a 'trading post empire' around Africa and throughout the Indian Ocean?
The Portuguese were largely able to establish dominance in the Indian Ocean because their ships were equipped with what?
incorrect: Exclusive knowledge of the monsoon winds

questions.text: The Portuguese focused on which primary objective once they inserted themselves into the Indian Ocean Trade Network?
correct: Owning and controlling it by force
incorrect: Participating peacefully as one merchant among many
Exploiting the natural resources of the hinterlands

questions.text: The typical ships of the average Indian Ocean merchant were described as being what, making them vulnerable to the Portuguese?
incorrect: Difficult to maneuver in open water

questions.text: What type of ship, along with the caravel, was loaded with cannons by the Portuguese to establish their dominance?
The Spanish, early on, set up their base of operations in which Pacific island chain?
The key difference between the Spanish method and the Portuguese method in empire building was that the Spanish established what?
Which three tactics did the Spanish use in their colonial holdings throughout the Indian Ocean, just as they did in the Americas?
correct: Tribute systems Taxation and coerced labor

questions.text: The Spanish colonial model was based on running full colonies, which allowed for extensive use of which economic and labor structures?
Which European state displaced the Portuguese to become the 'kings of the Indian Ocean trade'?
The Dutch used 'fancy schmancy' ships called what to gain their trade dominance?
The Dutch, like the Portuguese, used which methods to establish dominance and control over the Trade Network?
correct: Military force and control of trading posts
incorrect: Peaceful establishment of large agricultural colonies

questions.text: The Dutch applied their colonial model of trade control primarily to which Southeast Asian region?
Which European state would later end up controlling the largest sea-based empire in the world but had initial trouble establishing dominance?
The British initially lacked sufficient military power to take India from which powerful South Asian empire?
The British initially satisfied themselves in India by setting up what along the coast?
incorrect: Naval bases for the entire fleet

questions.text: Toward the end of the 18th century, the British gradually transformed their presence in India into what type of rule?
Despite European intrusion, which four groups of merchants continued to use the Indian Ocean Trade Network, illustrating continuity?
correct: Middle Eastern South Asian East Asian and Southeast Asian Merchants
incorrect: Mediterranean and West African Merchants only

questions.text: European entrance into the Indian Ocean Trade Network led to increased profits for whom?
correct: Both Europeans and many long-standing merchants
incorrect: Only the Ottoman and Safavid Empires

questions.text: Which long-established merchant group continued to make use of the Indian Ocean trade even while Europeans sought to dominate it?
The continued use of the Indian Ocean trade by states like the Mughal Empire led to what outcome for them?
incorrect: Led to their complete isolation from global trade
Forced them to align with the Spanish

questions.text: The Indian Ocean Trade Network demonstrated a continuity of use by whom even after European intrusion?
Which Asian state resisted European intrusion after being united under a Shogun from the Tokugawa Clan by the early 1600s?
The Shogun initially allowed trade with Europeans but quickly realized they posed a threat to what hard-won achievement?
By the second half of the 16th century, the conversion of many Japanese people to Christianity seemed to the Shogun like a recipe for what?
The Shogun responded to the Christian threat by expelling all Christian missionaries and taking what action toward the faith within Japan?
correct: Suppressing the faith often with violence
incorrect: Negotiating a peace treaty with the Pope
Allowing it to be practiced in designated areas
Integrating it into the traditional Shinto religion

questions.text: The resistance in Tokugawa Japan stemmed from the fact that European merchants often sought to do what besides buy and sell goods?
correct: Convert those various peoples to Christianity
incorrect: Buy up all the available farmland

questions.text: Before the Tokugawa Clan achieved unity, Japan had been weakened by what condition?
Which Chinese dynasty demonstrated resistance to European maritime dominance in the early 1500s?
One of the most important motives for the earlier Voyages of Zheng He was to create a situation where most maritime trade was processed through which state?
The ultimate result of Ming China's resistance to European maritime intrusion was what kind of trade policies?
When the Portuguese came to China in the early 1500s to trade, they were only able to do so through what questionable means?
incorrect: A formal treaty with the Emperor
A religious conversion of the Ming officials

questions.text: After Ming officials found out about the Portuguese tactics, they responded by taking what action?
incorrect: Formed an alliance with them against the Japanese
Limited them to a single port in the north

questions.text: The Ming isolationist trade policies largely shut down what type of Chinese trade?
The Assante Empire was a key trading partner in West Africa with the Portuguese and later which other European state?
The Assante Empire provided which three highly desired goods to their European trading partners?
The economic partnership with European states made the Assante 'stupid rich,' enabling them to expand what, thereby consolidating their power?
incorrect: Their diplomatic ties to the Ottomans

questions.text: The Assante later used their military power to repel which European state from colonizing the region for a long time?
The growth of the Assante Empire demonstrates how the new trade networks could promote what for participating African states?
correct: The growth of their power and wealth
incorrect: A decline in their indigenous cultures

questions.text: Which African state, located in the South, made strong diplomatic ties to Portuguese traders?
The Portuguese were highly desirous of obtaining which three commodities from the Kingdom of the Congo?
To further facilitate the economic relationship with Portugal, the King of the Congo and most of the nobles did what?
incorrect: Adopted Portuguese as the official language
Allowed Portugal to annex their coastline

questions.text: The economic connection between Portugal and the Kingdom of the Congo massively enriched whom, despite the later deterioration of the relationship?
The Kingdom of the Congo's relationship with Portugal highlights how some African states initially used European trade to achieve what?
incorrect: A switch to a peaceful Buddhist religion
A rejection of all European technology

questions.text: In the Americas, Colonial economies were largely structured around what primary activity?
To keep the agricultural economy running in the Americas, Europeans made use of what two main sources of labor systems?
correct: Existing labor systems and new ones

questions.text: Which existing labor system did the Spanish make use of in the Americas?
The Inca developed the Mit'a system, which required subjects of the empire to provide labor for what type of projects for a certain number of days per year?
incorrect: The individual benefit of local governors

questions.text: The Spanish 'pooped their pantaloons' when they discovered what buried in the hills of the Americas?
The Spanish primarily implemented the Mit'a system for which massive colonial operation?
The Spanish adoption of the Mit'a system for mining demonstrates a pattern of imperial powers utilizing what from pre-existing systems?
The Mit'a system, when adapted by the Spanish, was a method to ensure what necessary resource for their mining operations?
The original Inca Mit'a system required labor for state projects, while the Spanish version forced indigenous people to labor for what entity?
Which new labor system involved the transportation of millions of enslaved Africans throughout the Americas, mainly for plantation work?
The term 'chattel' means that the enslaved laborers were owned as what?
correct: Any other piece of property was owned
incorrect: A member of the owner's extended family

questions.text: What was a new aspect of this chattel slavery arrangement, besides the race-based component?
incorrect: It was limited to domestic servants
It was focused only on military service
It was a temporary condition of seven years

questions.text: The race-based component of Atlantic slavery was used to justify what?
incorrect: The high cost of imported labor

questions.text: To be identified as black in the Americas was to be considered what, according to the text, which cleared the conscience of plantation owners?
The children of enslaved people in the Americas inherited what status?
Which new labor system required a laborer to sign a contract binding them to work for a period of time, usually seven years?
Many poorer Europeans entered indentured servitude to pay for what?
incorrect: Their freedom from debt in Europe
A plot of land in the Americas

questions.text: After an indentured servant's contract was up, they were free to do what?
incorrect: Were forced to sign a new contract

questions.text: The indentured servitude system was based on what key legal document?
The encomienda system was a form of labor cooked up by which European imperial power?
The encomienda system was used to coerce which population into working for colonial authorities?
Indigenous people were forced to provide labor for the Spanish under the encomienda system in exchange for what two things?
The encomienda system was similar in a lot of ways to the old system of what in Europe?
Despite being called a form of labor in exchange for protection, the encomienda system was materially similar to what other labor system?
Which new labor system involved large agricultural estates owned by Elite Spaniards in the Americas?
Indigenous laborers under the hacienda system were forced to work in which location?
The crops grown on haciendas were primarily exported and sold on what type of market?
The primary focus of the encomienda system was controlling the population, while the hacienda system focused on what?
The hacienda system was another labor 'gem' cooked up by which European power?
On the continuity side, the African slave trade was a regular feature in which two established trade networks before the rise of Maritime Empires?
correct: The Mediterranean and Indian Ocean networks
incorrect: The Silk Road and Trans-Saharan networks
The North Sea and Baltic Sea networks

questions.text: In the Islamic world, the majority of enslaved Africans became what type of laborers?
In the Islamic world, the demand for which demographic of enslaved people was very high for domestic roles?
In some cases in the Islamic world, enslaved people could hold what two significant positions?
Enslaved people in the Indian Ocean and Mediterranean networks were often assimilated into what of the cultures into which they were sold?
The continuity of African slave trade realities existed during which phase of global history?
correct: The rise and establishment of Maritime Empires
incorrect: The fall of the Roman Empire

questions.text: The main economic engine of Imperial Empires in the Americas was difficult agricultural work, leading Europeans to purchase slaves of which gender at a two to one ratio?
The gender imbalance in the transatlantic slave trade significantly impacted what in various African states?
The shift in demand toward male slaves for plantation work in the Americas represents a significant change compared to the high demand for women and girls in which earlier context?
correct: Domestic service in the Islamic world
incorrect: Military positions in the Ottoman Empire
Building the Great Wall of China

questions.text: Over the course of about 350 years, how many Africans were sold to plantation owners in the Americas via the transatlantic slave trade?
The size of the transatlantic slave trade was far more massive than which two of its earlier counterparts?
incorrect: The Caribbean and South American internal trades

questions.text: The scale of the transatlantic slave trade represents a major what compared to earlier African slave trades?
What was the most distinctive change in the Atlantic slave trade in the Americas?
incorrect: The shift to using female labor
The implementation of a maximum contract length

questions.text: In the Americas, slavery became identified with 'Blackness', which provided the justification for what?
incorrect: The development of new sailing routes
The abolition of all other labor systems

questions.text: The racial component meant that plantation owners could treat their workers with violence while maintaining what?
Slavery becoming hereditary and race-based are two fundamental components that distinguish the Atlantic system from which older forms of slavery?
Which state's attempts at resistance resulted in isolationist trade policies that largely shut down sea-based trade?
The Spanish established their first major colonial base of operations in the Philippines while the Portuguese focused on what structure in the Indian Ocean?
The establishment of centralized authority by European monarchs was a prerequisite for the state-sponsored nature of what major historical event?
The Kingdom of the Congo enriched itself by trading what two specific metals with the Portuguese?
The new labor system that was temporary and involved a contract to pay for passage to the colonies was what?
The encomienda system and the hacienda system were both labor mechanisms developed and implemented by which European power?
The Dutch were able to take over as kings of the Indian Ocean trade due to their use of what specific type of vessel?
The demographic change in the transatlantic slave trade saw a high demand for male slaves due to the focus on which type of work in the Americas?
The rise of seab-based empires in Europe marked a significant what in the global balance of power?
The global shift in the balance of power during this period was from which type of empire to the sea-based European empires?
The adoption of which four maritime technologies primarily gave Europeans the edge in world power?
correct: Magnetic compass Astrolabe Latine sail and Improved astronomical charts
incorrect: Gunpowder Printing press Lateen sail and Galleon
Steam engines Telescopes and Standardized timekeeping

questions.text: The magnetic compass, a navigational tool adopted by Europeans, was first developed in which country?
The astrolabe, used to determine latitude and longitude by measuring stars, was first developed by which two groups?

Which type of charts, mainly the responsibility of Muslims and built on Greek work, helped sailors know their exact location?
The crucial point about these navigational technologies is that Europeans did what, rather than inventing them?
incorrect: Purchased them from Asian merchants at high cost
Redesigned them using only European concepts

questions.text: Europeans were exposed to these tools and innovations mainly through what activity along major trade routes?
The context that facilitated the transfer of these technologies to Europe was, in no small part, thanks to what period of stability?
The Portuguese Caravel was intentionally designed to be what size compared to older ships like the Chinese Junk?
The Caravel's smaller size gave it which two key advantages?
correct: More nimble and able to enter shallow coastal areas
incorrect: Faster speed and larger cargo capacity
Heavier armor and a permanent cannon deck
Ability to sail against the wind and larger crew quarters

questions.text: The Caravel was equipped with what, allowing it to function as a good fighting ship in addition to its agility?
The Portuguese developed the Carrack, a much larger ship, because their dreams of empire would need to be built on what foundation?
Compared to the Caravel, the Carrack was larger and could carry more of what two things?
The Carrack's ability to carry more guns was key to Portugal's reign in the Indian Ocean trade during which historical period?
Which European state's ship, the Fluyt, was primarily responsible for eventually Dethroning the Portuguese in the Indian Ocean trade?
The Dutch Fluyt was designed exclusively for what primary purpose?
The Fluyt had what two design features that made it highly efficient for trade?
correct: Massive cargo holds and required a smaller crew
incorrect: Heavily armored and could carry more cannons
Sleek design and exceptionally high speed
Self-propelled engine and a large hospital wing

questions.text: The Dutch cut the cost of Fluyt production by approximately half through the use of what method?
incorrect: Importing cheap wood from the Americas

questions.text: By the mid-17th century, Dutch Fluyts were responsible for about half of all Europe's what?

Topic_42_Economic_Social_and_Cultural_Ef.name: Topic 4.2: Economic, Social, and Cultural Effects of Maritime Empires (New Content)

questions.text: What was the dominant economic system in Europe during this period, emphasizing the buildup of mineral wealth?
Mercantilism defined a state's wealth primarily by which type of resources?
Mercantilist economies viewed the world's wealth as having what characteristic, leading to competition?
correct: A limited resource like a pie
incorrect: An infinite resource that could be constantly expanded
A resource controlled entirely by the church
A resource only accessible through military conquest

questions.text: In order to achieve a favorable balance of trade, mercantilist economies strove to have more of what than the other?
Mercantilism motivated the establishment of colonies because they created a kind of closed market to purchase what from the parent country?
Which type of business was a limited liability business, often chartered by the state, and funded by a group of investors?
When a business is said to have "limited liability," what does that mean for its investors?
correct: Investors could only lose the money they invested
incorrect: Investors were liable for all debts of the company
Investors had no say in the company's management

questions.text: Joint-stock companies became the main tool by which which two groups became intimately tied in a mutual interdependence for Imperial expansion?
Which joint-stock company was chartered in 1602 by the Dutch State and granted a monopoly on trade in the Indian Ocean?
Which two European states primarily funded their trade and Imperial ventures through the state, a reason why their influence began to wane?
What was the "big change" in the trade network during this period, which described the movement of goods, wealth, and laborers between the Eastern and Western Hemisphere?
correct: The rise of the Atlantic system of trade
incorrect: The expansion of the Silk Roads
The decline of the Indian Ocean Network
The establishment of the Mediterranean Sea League

questions.text: What agricultural good, grown on colonial plantations especially in the Caribbean, was of special importance in the exchange of goods?
What form of wealth, heavily exploited from mines like Potosí in modern-day Bolivia, was transported back to Spain and injected into the wider European economy?
The flow of silver satisfied a growing demand in which Asian country, further developing the commercialization of its economy?
The Atlantic system's coerced labor came in which three forms?
correct: Forcing Indigenous people to work Indentured servants and Enslaved Africans
incorrect: Free wage labor Serfdom and Bartering
Military conscription Trade guilds and Monastic orders

questions.text: Despite European Naval superiority in the Indian Ocean, what was a significant continuity in the network?
correct: All the various Merchants continued to trade and even benefited from The increased Merchant traffic
incorrect: The Silk Roads became the only viable trade route
European states seized all profits from Asian merchants
All trade in the region was replaced by military patrols

questions.text: Despite growing European dominance on the sea, which routes were still almost entirely controlled by various Asian land-based powers like Ming China and the Qing Dynasty?
correct: Overland routes like the Silk Roads

questions.text: What two types of labor continued and intensified during this time, with increased production for distant markets?
incorrect: Industrial factory work and Technological research

questions.text: Peasant farmers in South Asia increased their production for export due to the increasing European demand for which good?
Artisans increased their production of goods like Silk clothing from China and rugs from the Middle East due to what?
incorrect: A decline in European military power
A switch to democracy in Asia

questions.text: The social effect of the African slave trade, where a vast majority of enslaved laborers purchased were men, led to what demographic issue?
The depletion of the male population in West African states due to the slave trade led to an increase in which social practice?
correct: Polygyny the practice of men marrying more than one woman

questions.text: Which type of new languages were developed in the Americas as a synthesis of European, African, and indigenous languages?
Which two European states sent Catholic missionaries to their colonies in South America, using the church as an instrument to spread Christianity?
In some cases, indigenous groups practiced Christianity outwardly but privately continued to practice their own beliefs, which is an example of what?
correct: Syncretism a blending of belief systems

questions.text: Which Spanish priest made great efforts to protect indigenous Americans from the abuses of colonial authorities?
The efforts of missionaries like Bartolomé de las Casas led to new legislation outlawing what and limiting forms of coerced labor?
Enslaved Africans brought their native belief systems, including which major religion, that further led to blending in the Americas?
What is the state-driven economic system that emphasizes mineral wealth accumulation through a favorable balance of trade?
In mercantilism, what two specific resources were primarily considered a state's 'mineral wealth'?
A 'favorable balance of trade' required a mercantilist state to have more of what than imports?
How did colonies contribute to a mercantilist system?
correct: They created a closed market to purchase exports, sending wealth back
incorrect: They were required to pay the Gia tax
They independently managed their own foreign trade

questions.text: What type of business was a limited liability venture, funded by private investors, and often granted trade monopolies by the state?
What does 'limited liability' mean for investors in joint-stock companies?
correct: They could only lose the money that they invested in the business
incorrect: They were guaranteed to make a profit
They were fully liable for all company debts
They were restricted to trading only in Europe

questions.text: The state granting trade monopolies to joint-stock companies created a relationship of mutual what between the state and its merchants?
Which major joint-stock company was chartered by the Dutch state in 1602 with a monopoly on trade in the Indian Ocean?
The rivalry between the French, British, and Dutch joint-stock companies contributed to what major conflict?
Which two European powers primarily funded their imperial ventures through the state, a factor contributing to their waning influence?
What major new trade network describes the movement of goods, wealth, and laborers between the Eastern and Western Hemispheres?
What specific cash crop, grown on colonial plantations (especially in the Caribbean), was of 'special importance' in the Atlantic system?
What mineral was 'King' in the transfer of wealth across the Atlantic System?
Name the massive silver mine heavily exploited by the Spanish in modern-day Bolivia.
The flow of American silver to Asia contributed to the further development of what aspect of the Chinese economy?
Name two types of goods purchased in Asian markets with silver and then traded across the Atlantic System.
The labor utilized in the burgeoning Atlantic economy was largely in what general form?
The Spanish attempted to force which specific group in their colonial holdings to work for them?
The British initially began by using which labor system before turning primarily to enslaved Africans?
What two factors established and maintained the Atlantic System of trade?
correct: Global flow of silver and trade monopolies
incorrect: Gold flow and universal free trade
The end of all Asian trade and labor unions

questions.text: Despite European naval superiority, what continuity existed in the Indian Ocean Network during this period?
correct: Various Afro-Eurasian Merchants continued to trade and benefited
incorrect: European companies gained exclusive trade rights
All trade shifted to the Atlantic System

questions.text: Who still maintained almost entire control over the Overland routes like the Silk Roads?
correct: Various Asian land-based Powers (e.g., Ming/Qing China)

questions.text: What type of farmers grew only what they needed to survive, though increasing demand led them to produce more for distant markets?
Increasing European demand for which crop caused peasant farmers in South Asia to increase their production for export?
What are skilled laborers who made goods by hand, and whose production increased due to European demand for silk and rugs?
What significant social effect resulted in West Africa from the African slave trade, due to the high demand for intensive agriculture?
correct: A gender imbalance (vast majority of enslaved were men)

questions.text: The depletion of the male population in West African states led to an increase in the practice of men marrying more than one woman, known as:
In the Americas, the synthesis of European, African, and sometimes Indigenous languages led to the development of what type of new language?
What term describes the cultural mixing of various African cultures and languages with European and Indigenous ones in the Americas?
The Spanish and Portuguese sent what group to their colonies, using the Church as an instrument to spread Christianity?
What technology did the Catholic Church use 'prodigiously' to spread European language and culture rapidly throughout their colonial holdings?
When indigenous groups in the Americas adopted Christianity outwardly but practiced their own religion privately, this was an example of:
What was the response of colonial authorities when they discovered indigenous groups were still practicing their own religious beliefs in private?
Name the Dominican priest who made efforts to protect Indigenous Americans from the abuses of colonial authorities.
The efforts of Bartolomé de las Casas led to new legislation outlawing the enslavement of which group?
What major world religion did enslaved Africans bring with them that contributed to further cultural blending in the Americas?
In the mercantilist view, why was the world's wealth described as 'like a pie'?
correct: The world's mineral wealth was considered a limited, finite resource
incorrect: It was meant to be shared equally by all nations
It was easily expandable through colonization
It was only accessible through baking

questions.text: The growth of joint-stock companies is an innovation in how businesses were funded, primarily being funded by:
correct: Private funds / a group of investors

questions.text: The Dutch East India Company became exceedingly rich by edging out which rival European power in the Indian Ocean network?
The massive injection of American silver into the wider European economy was primarily used for what purpose?
incorrect: To pay for the Boyars' land

questions.text: The continuity of trade meant that existing merchants in the Indian Ocean Network benefited from what factor?
incorrect: The end of all European influence

questions.text: Increasing demand for goods facilitated by new connections led to an increase in the peasant farmers' what, in many cases?
Why was the vast majority of enslaved labor purchased for the Americas male?
correct: The wealth was driven by highly intensive agriculture (sugar cane)
incorrect: Women were outlawed from being enslaved
Men had higher rates of immunity to European diseases
Women were primarily used in silver mining

questions.text: The imposition of European language and culture upon indigenous populations in South America was primarily facilitated by which institution?
The economic rivalry that led to the Anglo-Dutch War was rooted in the competition for trade monopolies granted by the state to whom?
If a mercantilist state was importing goods, what was the flow of mineral wealth in relation to the state?
correct: Gold and silver flowed out of the state
incorrect: Gold and silver flowed into the state
The amount of gold and silver was unaffected

questions.text: What political concept were European states aiming for by turning themselves into the 'political and geographical equivalent' of the pie-hogging cousin, Kyle?
correct: Dominance and control of limited resources

questions.text: The efforts of Bartolomé de las Casas limited the forms of coerced labor for Indigenous Americans, reflecting what shift in colonial policy?
correct: An attempt to curb the abuses of colonial authorities
incorrect: An endorsement of the Gia tax

questions.text: What type of labor continued and intensified in both South Asia and China during this period to meet European demand for goods like cotton and silk?
The fundamental reason the Atlantic System of trade had not existed prior to 1492 was the lack of connection between which two geographical areas?
incorrect: The Mediterranean and the Indian Ocean

questions.text: The state-driven economic system that saw the world's wealth as 'like a pie' with finite resources was known as:
Mercantilist economies were primarily focused on the buildup of what specific type of wealth?
Why did a favorable balance of trade require more exports than imports under mercantilism?
correct: Exports brought mineral wealth (gold/silver) into the country
incorrect: Imports generated the highest tax revenue

questions.text: The ultimate goal of mercantilist economic strategy was to obtain and consolidate what for the imperial parent country?
Mercantilism served as a powerful motivation for establishing and growing empires because colonies created what for the parent country's exports?
What business innovation allowed investors to fund high-risk, high-reward ventures like imperial expansion without being personally ruined if the venture failed?
How did the state primarily support and encourage the expansion efforts of joint-stock companies?
correct: By granting them trade monopolies in various regions
incorrect: By converting to the company's religion

questions.text: The establishment of joint-stock companies was a big innovation because they were funded by whom, rather than the state?
The mutual interdependence between the state and merchants meant the state relied on merchants to expand its influence, while merchants relied on the state for what?
correct: To keep their interest and activity safe

questions.text: What was the main tool by which the mutual arrangement between the state and merchants led to expanding empires?
The Dutch East India Company was able to expand its power and influence by edging out which rival European power in the Indian Ocean network?
What was one significant reason for the waning influence of Spain and Portugal on the world stage during this period, compared to the British and Dutch?
correct: They mainly funded their trade and imperial ventures through the state
incorrect: They lacked a favorable balance of trade
They focused solely on Asia, ignoring the Americas
They embraced limited liability too early

questions.text: Mercantilism created a 'profound competition' between European states due to the belief that the world's mineral wealth was what?
Which three European states developed joint-stock companies for purposes of trade and imperial expansion, leading to growing rivalries?
What term describes the economic goal of wanting more gold and silver to come in than go out?
What does a state risk losing if it imports more goods than it exports under the mercantilist model?
The growth of the Dutch East India Company led to the investors becoming exceedingly rich, primarily due to their monopoly on trade in which region?
The ability for a government to approve a joint-stock business and grant it special rights is known as being:
The Atlantic System of trade describes the movement of what three elements between the Eastern and Western Hemispheres?
The Atlantic System of trade originated after the voyage of which explorer in 1492?
The abundance of sugar from colonial plantations caused the price of sugar to do what in Europe, while demand did what?
incorrect: Both prices and demand decreased sharply
Both prices and demand increased sharply

questions.text: The massive silver mine exploited by the Spanish in Potosí was located in what modern-day South American country?

correct: The trade of Asian goods (Silk/Porcelain) across the Atlantic system

The conversion of China to Christianity

questions.text: What was the term for labor that was compelled or forced upon workers, common in the Atlantic economy?

correct: Global flow of silver and trade monopolies granted by heads of state
incorrect: The printing press and the Catholic Church
The Reconquista and the Gia tax

questions.text: Colonial plantations in the Caribbean specialized in the growth of sugar cane, leading to high demand for which group of coerced laborers?

correct: The trade of Asian goods across the Atlantic system
incorrect: The collapse of the Qing Dynasty
The rise of absolutism in Europe
The end of the Indian Ocean Trade

questions.text: In the Atlantic System, the transfer of wealth from Potosí mines mainly involved which two continents at the outset?

The high demand for sugar in Europe was driven by the sweet goods' decreasing what?

incorrect: North America, Russia, and the Middle East
Europe, Africa, and the Middle East

questions.text: Who granted the trade monopolies that were essential to maintaining the Atlantic system?

The Spanish heavily relied on forced indigenous labor, indentured servitude was used by the British initially, but ultimately, all imperial powers majored on which group for labor?
A significant continuity during this period was the continued flourishing and increasing reach of which type of markets across Afro-Eurasia?

correct: Various Merchants (from the Middle East to Southeast Asia)

questions.text: The continuing success of the regional markets in Afro-Eurasia happened right at the same time as what major European activity?
correct: Europeans were increasingly dominating the Indian Ocean Network
incorrect: The fall of the Ottoman Empire
The start of the Industrial Revolution
The end of all Asian trade

questions.text: Overland routes like the Silk Roads were still almost entirely controlled by various Asian land-based powers, most notably which two Chinese dynasties?
correct: Ming China and the Qing Dynasty

questions.text: The continuity of labor during this time meant that most people in the world continued to work as what, growing what they needed to survive?
The increasing demand for goods facilitated by new connections caused peasants to produce more agricultural goods for what type of markets?

The increase in peasant production for distant markets led to an increase in their what, in many cases?
Despite European Naval superiority in the Indian Ocean, their advantage was primarily in ships and what other area?
The continuation of peasant and artisan labor during this time is an example of what historical concept in a period of great change?
Peasant farmers were mainly which type of farmer, meaning they grew only what they needed to survive?
The cultural group that gained more wealth from the sale of Silk clothing to Europeans were the artisans of which state?
Which geographic area saw an increase in artisan production of rugs due to European demand?
Increased European dominance on the sea had what effect on the Overland routes?
correct: They were still almost entirely controlled by various Asian land-based Powers
incorrect: They became controlled by the Dutch East India Company
They shifted entirely to water routes

questions.text: The vast majority of enslaved laborers purchased were men, primarily driven by the intensive nature of which type of agriculture in the Americas?
What social effect of the African slave trade was the result of West African states being depleted of their male population?
The gender imbalance in West African states led to an increase in what family structure practice?
correct: Polygyny (men marrying more than one woman)

questions.text: In the Americas, what was the term for the mixed languages that developed as a synthesis of European, African, and sometimes indigenous languages?
Enslaved Africans ceased speaking their own native languages and adopted Creole languages within approximately what timeframe?
